No separate print layout required."}],"printHookCode":{"prePrint":"","print":"","postPrint":""},"noReportsNeeded":true}
#6 — Generate README.md (18:50:27 | sent: 75,608 chars | received: 16,007 chars)
✉ Prompt Sent (75,608 chars) 
[SYSTEM]
You are a technical documentation writer. Given a blueprint document and analysis results,
create a detailed README.md that explains:
1. A summary of the blueprint's business requirements
2. How the blueprint was translated into code (methodology)
3. Code flow — how SBR hooks, CBR hooks, and templates interact
4. Design patterns used (manager classes, delegation, etc.)
5. File-by-file breakdown of what was generated and why
6. Suggestions for testing, deployment, and future enhancements
7. Any caveats or manual steps needed

Write in clear markdown with headers, bullet points, and code references where helpful.
Do NOT include markdown code fences around the entire response — return raw markdown directly.

[USER]
=== BLUEPRINT DOCUMENT ===
   MARKEN BLUEPRINTING Phase 1 ShipExec Design September 6, 2023 Scott Spencer Syr1sxs@ups.com 
Contents General Company Level Configuration .................................................................................................... 2 UPS Adapter Configuration .................................................................................................................. 2 CS Adapter Configuration ..................................................................................................................... 2 Phase 1 Use Case 1 and 2 - Specimen Returns Configuration .................................................................. 2 Configurable ShipExec Server Business Rules Values ......................................................................... 2 Reference Field Mapping ...................................................................................................................... 2 Profile Field Options ............................................................................................................................. 2 User Configuration ................................................................................................................................ 3 Custom Templates ..................................................................................................................................... 4 Shipping Template – Biological Returns Shipping Template ............................................................... 4 Client Side Business Rules ................................................................................................................... 4 CBR PageLoaded .................................................................................................................................. 4 CBR NewShipment ............................................................................................................................... 4 CBR PreShip ......................................................................................................................................... 4 Server Side Business Rules ................................................................................................................... 4 SBR PreShip ......................................................................................................................................... 4 SBR Ship ............................................................................................................................................... 5    
General Company Level Configuration  UPS Adapter Configuration UserID:  MarkenShipExec Password:  APIapi2023!! Access Key: ED6F5B10202ADED2  CS Adapter Configuration WorldEase configuration and POE data (to be done in separate phase)  Phase 1 Use Case 1 and 2 - Specimen Returns Configuration  Configurable ShipExec Server Business Rules Values  NA  Reference Field Mapping • ConsigneeReference – “Temperature” with Validation list values: • Frozen • Ambient • Refrigerated • Ambient/Refrigerated Combo Box • ShipperReference – “Study Reference Code” • From User level Custom2 • MiscReference1 – “Protocol Number” • From User level Custom1 • MiscReference2 – “Site Number” • From User level Custom3 • MiscReference3 – “Dry Ice Weight (kg)” • User entered value • For use with Dry Ice Shipments. • Hidden by default, only visible when “Temperature” is selected as “Frozen” • MiscReference4 – “Biological Sample”  • User selected value • Boolean true/false • Defaulted to true/on • New checkbox on screen near ‘Saturday Delivery’ checkbox  Profile Field Options  Rename  o ‘Consignee’ tab to ‘Pickup From’ o Consignee Reference to ‘Temperature’ using validation list (Return From CA to US User Profile)  Frozen  Ambient 
 Refrigerated  Ambient/Refrigerated Combo Box o Consignee Reference to ‘Temperature’ using validation list (Return From EU to UK User Profile)  Ambient  Refrigerated o MiscReference1 to ‘Protocol Number’ o MiscReference2 to ‘Site Number’ o MiscReference3 to ‘Dry Ice Weight (kg)’ o MiscReference4 to ‘Biological Sample’ o Shipper Reference to ‘Study Reference Guide’  Hide o BrokerageThirdPartyBilling o Box Type o Carrier Instructions o Consignee PO Box o Consignee Third Party Billing o Third Party Billing o Declared Value Amount o Dimensions o Insurance Method o Origin Address o Packaging o Piece Count o Tracking Number o Residential Indicator o PO Box Indicator o Ship Date o Account Number o Tax ID  Default o Description – ‘UN3373 Category B Human Sample’ o Shipper – Set at the Site Profile level o Dry Ice Weight Units – ‘KG’ o Return Address – Set at the Site Profile level o Return Delivery – True o Saturday Delivery – True o Service – UPS Express o Terms – Prepaid o Weight Unit – KG (Use Case 2 EU to UK Profile only)  User Configuration  Address data from user spreadsheet o Will serve as pickup address for return labels  Custom1 value will serve as Protocol Number saved in Shipper Reference  Custom2 value will serve as Study Reference Internal Code saved in MiscReference1  Custom3 value will serve as Site Number saved in MiscReference2  
Custom Templates Shipping Template – Biological Returns Shipping Template  Hide Rate Button  Client Side Business Rules CBR PageLoaded  On login, user should automatically be taken to the shipping page.  When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is selected, set the package weight based on the option selected: o Ambient – 3  o Frozen – 6  o Refrigerated – 5  o Ambient/Refrigerated Combo Box – 6   When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is set to ‘Frozen’, have user enter in the required ‘Dry Ice Weight’ either in new modal or field to capture and store in MiscReference3.  This will then be used to populate the Shipment Request Dry Ice values during SBR PreShip.  When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is any other value besides ‘Frozen’, ‘Dry Ice Weight’ should not be editable  When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label  When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button  CBR NewShipment  Set Consignee address to User address values o This will be the ‘Pickup From’ for Returns  Set Shipper Reference/Study Reference Code to User Custom2 value  Set MiscReference1/Protocol Number to User Custom1 value  When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label  CBR PreShip  Recommended…when the Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada automatically click the ‘Pickup Request’ button and ‘Save’ so that the Pickup object is associated with the Shipment.  Default values are saved in Profile Field Options.  NOTE: If there are issues with this working correctly from the client side, we will need to develop a server side strategy using the Pickup object associated to the SBR Ship method and additional User Custom values (see SBR Ship).  Server Side Business Rules  SBR PreShip  For all return labels moving internationally (consignee/pickup from and return to countries are different), turn on paperless invoice by setting package level commercial invoice method to 1 and setting package level export reason to “Medical”.   Adapter Selection o Default to use the CS Adapter services 
o If MiscReference4/Biological Sample value is ‘true’, we need to use the CSAdapter services for rate shop and final shipping/processing.  Domestic US to US  o Validate NDA Early AM by rate shopping.  If not valid, switch to NDA without Saturday Delivery  International (CA to CA, any other cross border movement)  o Validate UPS Express with Saturday Delivery by rate shopping.  If not valid, switch to UPS Saver without Saturday Delivery.  Biological Sample Setting - if MiscReference4/Biological Sample value is ‘true’  Set the package level PackageExtras  “RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE” set to “32”  If Dry Ice Weight/MiscReference3 is not null o Set Dry Ice Weight = MiscReference3 o Set Dry Ice Regulation Set…  If shipment is US to US, make sure Dry Ice Regulation Set is set to “International Air Transportation Association regulations.”  If shipment is not US to US, make sure Dry Ice Regulation Set is “US 49 CFR regulations.” o Make sure Dry Ice Purpose is set to “Medical.” o Add the Dry Ice Weight from MiscReference3 to the current Package.Weight.Amount.   Convert the weight in MiscReference3 from KG to LBS first.   SBR Ship  BACKUP – If the CBR PreShip strategy to associate the Pickup to the Shipment by clicking the ‘Pickup Request’ button and ‘Save’ing the values does not work, we will need to develop a backup strategy to create a new Pickup object and populate it with configurable values from the User Custom data fields (see CBR PreShip)    


=== IMPLEMENTATION PLAN ===
{"summary":"The blueprint defines Phase 1 support for Marken biological returns shipping in ShipExec. The core requirements are to reconfigure the shipping UI for a returns workflow, rename and repurpose several reference fields, hide many standard shipping controls, default shipment values from user/site profile data, and add a new dry-ice input behavior that is only editable when Temperature is Frozen. The business flow also needs client-side automation for return-label pickup association, plus server-side validation and shipping logic to enforce international return rules, paperless invoice behavior, carrier selection strategy, and dry ice/package extra handling.\n\nOverall, this is a combined template + CBR + SBR implementation. The shipping template must be tailored to a “Pickup From” returns screen, while client business rules must handle page navigation, defaults, temperature-based field logic, and pickup button behavior. Server business rules must enforce the real shipping constraints so the workflow remains correct even if the client-side automation fails. The phase intentionally excludes CS/WorldEase/POE work, so implementation should focus only on the return-label flow and the UPS/CS adapter selection logic described here.","sbrPlan":[{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Enforce server-side returns shipping rules before shipment creation: detect international returns, enable paperless invoice and set export reason to Medical, choose adapter/service behavior based on Biological Sample and route type, validate/swap service to fallback options via rate shopping, and prepare dry-ice/package extra values from MiscReference fields."},{"method":"Ship","purpose":"Provide backup shipping behavior if the client-side pickup association fails; optionally create or attach a Pickup object from user custom/profile data before allowing the default ship flow, or override shipping only if the pickup association strategy cannot be completed client-side."},{"method":"PostShip","purpose":"Persist/shadow-log shipped return data if required and ensure any final response adjustments are compatible with the biological returns workflow; also a safe place to capture tracking/ship metadata for downstream systems if the blueprint later adds ERP writeback."},{"method":"PreRate","purpose":"Adjust or constrain rate-shopping services for the returns workflow, especially to support validating NDA Early AM or UPS Express with Saturday Delivery and to ensure the correct adapter/service set is considered before rating."},{"method":"PostRate","purpose":"Inspect rating results and apply service fallback decisions when the preferred return service is invalid, then surface the chosen service strategy to the client if needed."}],"cbrPlan":[{"method":"PageLoaded","purpose":"Auto-navigate to the shipping page on login, and when the shipping page is reached, initialize returns-specific UI state such as hiding/showing the Pickup button based on the consignee/pickup country and preparing temperature-related field behavior."},{"method":"NewShipment","purpose":"Default a new return shipment using user/site profile data: set the Consignee/Pickup From address from the user address data, prefill Shipper Reference from Custom2, MiscReference1 from Custom1, default shipper/service/terms/weight unit values, and auto-trigger pickup association when the address is non-Canada."},{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Perform client-side preparation immediately before shipping: if Pickup From is non-Canada, attempt to associate a pickup and save it before ship, and ensure any temperature/dry-ice values are present in the shipment request so the server receives them."},{"method":"PostLoad","purpose":"When a load or scan occurs, apply returns-specific UI updates such as setting package weight from Temperature selection, opening the dry-ice input prompt when Frozen is selected, preventing edits when Temperature is not Frozen, and hiding or disabling the Pickup button when the address is Canadian."},{"method":"Keystroke","purpose":"Support fast operator interaction on the shipping screen, potentially to react to temperature changes or force focus/submit behavior in the returns workflow without requiring manual mouse actions."},{"method":"PreBuildShipment","purpose":"As a safeguard, ensure the shipment object assembled from the UI contains the correct user-entered dry-ice value and any return-specific defaults before the object is posted to the server."}],"cbrPlan":[{"method":"PageLoaded","purpose":"Auto-navigate to the shipping page on login, and when the shipping page is reached, initialize returns-specific UI state such as hiding/showing the Pickup button based on the consignee/pickup country and preparing temperature-related field behavior."},{"method":"NewShipment","purpose":"Default a new return shipment using user/site profile data: set the Consignee/Pickup From address from the user address data, prefill Shipper Reference from Custom2, MiscReference1 from Custom1, default shipper/service/terms/weight unit values, and auto-trigger pickup association when the address is non-Canada."},{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Perform client-side preparation immediately before shipping: if Pickup From is non-Canada, attempt to associate a pickup and save it before ship, and ensure any temperature/dry-ice values are present in the shipment request so the server receives them."},{"method":"PostLoad","purpose":"When a load or scan occurs, apply returns-specific UI updates such as setting package weight from Temperature selection, opening the dry-ice input prompt when Frozen is selected, preventing edits when Temperature is not Frozen, and hiding or disabling the Pickup button when the address is Canadian."},{"method":"Keystroke","purpose":"Support fast operator interaction on the shipping screen, potentially to react to temperature changes or force focus/submit behavior in the returns workflow without requiring manual mouse actions."},{"method":"PreBuildShipment","purpose":"As a safeguard, ensure the shipment object assembled from the UI contains the correct user-entered dry-ice value and any return-specific defaults before the object is posted to the server."}],"templatePlan":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","changes":"Rename the Consignee tab to Pickup From via FieldOptions/caption overrides and ensure the shipping layout supports the returns workflow. Hide the Rate button per blueprint, hide the Pickup button when the destination country is Canada, and expose a dry-ice field or modal trigger tied to MiscReference3 only when Temperature is Frozen. Ensure the relevant fields (ConsigneeReference, ShipperReference, MiscReference1-4) are present and mapped to the renamed labels."},{"file":"rateResultModal.html","changes":"No structural change is required unless the template exposes service-selection actions that need to be visually suppressed for this workflow. If the product requires hiding rate selection entirely for the shipping screen, ensure any rate-related entry points from the shipping template do not navigate here."},{"file":"closeManifestTemplate.html","changes":"No blueprint-specific change identified for Phase 1."},{"file":"transmitTemplate.html","changes":"No blueprint-specific change identified for Phase 1."},{"file":"groupmanagerTemplate.html","changes":"No blueprint-specific change identified for Phase 1."}],"managerClasses":[{"name":"ReturnsShipmentManager","responsibility":"Encapsulate returns-specific shipment initialization, pickup association preparation, and profile/user data mapping for NewShipment and PreShip flows."},{"name":"TemperatureHandlingManager","responsibility":"Map ConsigneeReference/Temperature values to package weight defaults, control dry-ice editability, and translate frozen shipment inputs into shipment request fields."},{"name":"DryIceManager","responsibility":"Validate and convert dry-ice weight from MiscReference3 in KG to package-level values in LBS, and populate dry-ice purpose/regulation/package extras for the SBR PreShip hook."},{"name":"PickupAssociationManager","responsibility":"Determine whether Pickup must be associated, manage client-side/server-side fallback strategies, and prepare or create Pickup objects when the pickup button automation fails."},{"name":"ReturnsServiceSelectionManager","responsibility":"Apply the service validation and fallback rules for domestic/international returns, including UPS/CS adapter selection and Saturday-delivery fallback logic."}],"risks":["Client-side auto-clicking Save/Pickup may be unreliable in Thin Client timing, so the server-side backup pickup strategy in SBR Ship should be implemented defensively.","The blueprint mixes UI field renaming with underlying ShipExec property names; captions can change without breaking bindings, but property names and data mapping must remain consistent.","Dry-ice weight conversion from KG to LBS must be precise and should account for numeric parsing/rounding so package weight is not corrupted.","The international return rules rely on determining country pairs correctly; misclassification could incorrectly enable paperless invoice or the wrong service fallback.","Service validation by rate shopping can be slow or fail if carrier APIs are unavailable; the implementation should handle exceptions and choose safe fallback behavior.","Hidden fields in profile/FieldOptions may conflict with template-level visibility changes if both are applied inconsistently.","The blueprint does not define exact user-custom field shapes beyond Custom1/2/3 and address data, so mapping assumptions need confirmation before coding."],"implementationOrder":["Confirm/record the exact FieldOptions and profile defaults required for the returns workflow, including captions, hidden fields, and default values.","Update shippingTemplate.html and profile-driven captions/visibility so the UI reflects the Pickup From returns flow and hides disallowed controls like Rate and Pickup for Canada.","Implement CBR PageLoaded and NewShipment first to establish the UI defaults, address mapping, and country-based Pickup button behavior.","Implement CBR PreShip and any helper methods for attempting pickup association and passing dry-ice/user data forward.","Implement SBR PreShip to enforce the authoritative shipping rules: paperless invoice, export reason, adapter/service selection, dry-ice translation, and package extra setup.","Implement SBR Ship as the fallback pickup-association strategy if client automation does not reliably attach the Pickup object.","Add any supporting manager/helper classes for returns mapping, dry ice, service selection, and pickup handling, then wire them into SoxBusinessRules.","Perform end-to-end testing for Canada domestic returns, US/CA cross-border returns, and frozen dry-ice returns to verify UI behavior, field mapping, and server-side enforcement."]}

=== SBR ANALYSIS ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint requires only server-side enforcement and fallback logic in SBR for Phase 1. The authoritative shipping logic belongs in SBR PreShip because it must: detect international return labels, enable paperless invoice and set export reason to Medical, choose between CS and UPS service strategies, validate domestic/international service fallbacks by rate shopping, and apply biological sample/dry-ice package extras. The SBR Ship hook is only needed as a backup when the client-side pickup association fails; it should create or attach a Pickup object from user custom/profile data, but otherwise allow the default ship flow. PostShip, PreRate, and PostRate are not explicitly required by the blueprint as server hooks because the blueprint’s rate-shopping validation is part of the PreShip enforcement step, and there is no post-ship integration/writeback requirement defined for Phase 1. No BusinessRuleSettings keys are required because the blueprint explicitly says the configurable SBR values are NA. All business logic is encapsulated in helper classes, with SoxBusinessRules methods delegating only to the manager.","sbrMethods":{"PreShip":"var mgr = new ReturnsShipmentManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, Profile, BusinessRuleSettings, ClientContext); mgr.PreShip(shipmentRequest, userParams);","Ship":"var mgr = new ReturnsShipmentManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, Profile, BusinessRuleSettings, ClientContext); return mgr.Ship(shipmentRequest, pickup, shipWithoutTransaction, print, userParams);"},"businessRuleSettings":[],"helperClasses":"using System;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Globalization;\nusing System.Linq;\nusing PSI.Sox;\nusing PSI.Sox.Api;\nusing PSI.Sox.Client;\n\n/*\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * File: ReturnsShipmentManager.cs\n * Purpose: Encapsulates all Phase 1 Marken return-label server-side logic so the\n *          SoxBusinessRules class remains a thin delegator. This manager enforces\n *          the blueprint requirements for international return handling, dry ice,\n *          biological sample extras, service fallback logic, and pickup fallback.\n * Architecture Notes:\n *   - PreShip is the authoritative validation/enforcement hook.\n *   - Ship is a defensive fallback that only creates/attaches Pickup when the\n *     client-side Pickup Request automation fails.\n *   - Additional helper methods keep the logic readable for junior developers.\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n */\npublic class ReturnsShipmentManager\n{\n    private readonly ILogger _logger;\n    private readonly IBusinessObjectApi _businessObjectApi;\n    private readonly IProfile _profile;\n    private readonly List<BusinessRuleSetting> _businessRuleSettings;\n    private readonly ClientContext _clientContext;\n    private readonly Tools _tools;\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Creates the returns shipping manager used by SBR hooks.\n    /// This constructor wires the ShipExec runtime dependencies into the helper.\n    /// </summary>\n    public ReturnsShipmentManager(ILogger logger, IBusinessObjectApi businessObjectApi, IProfile profile, List<BusinessRuleSetting> businessRuleSettings, ClientContext clientContext)\n    {\n        _logger = logger;\n        _businessObjectApi = businessObjectApi;\n        _profile = profile;\n        _businessRuleSettings = businessRuleSettings;\n        _clientContext = clientContext;\n        _tools = new Tools(logger);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Enforces the Marken Phase 1 return-label rules before shipment processing.\n    /// Blueprint requirements fulfilled here:\n    /// 1) International return labels get paperless invoice and Medical export reason.\n    /// 2) Biological Sample true enables CSAdapter-style processing behavior.\n    /// 3) Domestic and international service fallback decisions are validated.\n    /// 4) Dry ice weight in MiscReference3 is converted from KG to LBS and added.\n    /// 5) Biological sample package extras are applied.\n    /// </summary>\n    public void PreShip(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n    {\n        // Step 1: Validate the request shape early so we fail fast with a useful message.\n        if (shipmentRequest == null)\n            throw new Exception(\"ShipmentRequest is required for Marken return processing.\");\n\n        if (shipmentRequest.Packages == null || shipmentRequest.Packages.Count == 0)\n            throw new Exception(\"At least one package is required for Marken return processing.\");\n\n        // Step 2: Read the return workflow fields from the first package.\n        // The blueprint maps these fields by profile caption, but the underlying API\n        // still exposes them by their actual request properties.\n        PackageRequest pkg = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\n        string temperature = GetString(pkg.ConsigneeReference);\n        string biologicalSample = GetString(pkg.MiscReference4);\n        string dryIceKgText = GetString(pkg.MiscReference3);\n\n        // Step 3: Determine origin and destination countries for return-label logic.\n        string shipFromCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.Consignee);\n        string shipToCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.ShipperAddress);\n\n        bool isInternationalReturn = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipFromCountry)\n            && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipToCountry)\n            && !string.Equals(shipFromCountry, shipToCountry, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n        bool isUsToUs = string.Equals(shipFromCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n            && string.Equals(shipToCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n        bool isBiologicalSample = IsTrue(biologicalSample);\n\n        // Step 4: Turn on paperless invoice and Medical export reason for all international returns.\n        // The blueprint explicitly states this is required whenever consignee/pickup-from\n        // and return-to countries differ.\n        if (isInternationalReturn)\n        {\n            if (pkg.CommercialInvoice == null)\n                pkg.CommercialInvoice = new CommercialInvoice();\n\n            // Commercial invoice method 1 = paperless invoice per blueprint.\n            pkg.CommercialInvoice.Method = 1;\n            pkg.CommercialInvoice.ExportReason = \"Medical\";\n        }\n\n        // Step 5: Apply biological sample package extra required by the blueprint.\n        if (isBiologicalSample)\n        {\n            EnsurePackageExtras(pkg);\n            SetPackageExtra(pkg, \"RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE\", \"32\");\n        }\n\n        // Step 6: Apply dry ice logic when the user entered a value in MiscReference3.\n        // The input is defined as KG in the blueprint and must be converted to LBS\n        // before being added to the package weight.\n        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dryIceKgText))\n        {\n            decimal dryIceKg;\n            if (!decimal.TryParse(dryIceKgText, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out dryIceKg))\n                throw new Exception(\"Dry Ice Weight (kg) must be a numeric value.\");\n\n            decimal dryIceLbs = ConvertKgToLbs(dryIceKg);\n\n            EnsurePackageExtras(pkg);\n            SetPackageExtra(pkg, \"DRY_ICE_WEIGHT\", dryIceKg.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));\n            SetPackageExtra(pkg, \"DRY_ICE_PURPOSE\", \"Medical\");\n            SetPackageExtra(pkg, \"DRY_ICE_REGULATION_SET\", isUsToUs\n                ? \"International Air Transportation Association regulations.\"\n                : \"US 49 CFR regulations.\");\n\n            // Step 7: Add dry ice weight to the current package weight.\n            // We only touch the numeric amount if a weight object already exists;\n            // otherwise we create one so the shipment remains valid.\n            if (pkg.Weight == null)\n                pkg.Weight = new Weight();\n\n            decimal currentWeight = GetWeightAmount(pkg.Weight);\n            pkg.Weight.Amount = currentWeight + dryIceLbs;\n        }\n\n        // Step 8: Service validation / fallback strategy.\n        // The blueprint distinguishes between domestic US-to-US and international/cross-border.\n        // The rate shopping check is performed here because it must be authoritative.\n        if (isUsToUs)\n        {\n            ValidateOrFallbackService(shipmentRequest, pkg, \"NDA Early AM\", \"NDA without Saturday Delivery\");\n        }\n        else\n        {\n            ValidateOrFallbackService(shipmentRequest, pkg, \"UPS Express with Saturday Delivery\", \"UPS Saver without Saturday Delivery\");\n        }\n\n        // Step 9: Choose CS Adapter behavior when Biological Sample is true.\n        // The blueprint says to default to CS Adapter services, and definitely use them\n        // when Biological Sample is true. In ShipExec terms, the service selection above\n        // remains the source of truth; the service string can be adjusted here if needed.\n        if (isBiologicalSample)\n        {\n            // Keep this as a dedicated helper call so the intent is explicit.\n            EnsureCsAdapterCompatibleService(pkg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Defensive ship-time fallback for pickup association.\n    /// If client-side automation already attached the Pickup, this returns null and\n    /// the normal ShipExec carrier flow continues. If not, we create or attach a\n    /// Pickup object using user/profile data so the return-label workflow can still succeed.\n    /// </summary>\n    public ShipmentResponse Ship(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, Pickup pickup, bool shipWithoutTransaction, bool print, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n    {\n        // Step 1: Allow the default ShipExec flow whenever a pickup already exists.\n        if (pickup != null)\n            return null;\n\n        // Step 2: Determine whether this shipment needs pickup fallback handling.\n        if (shipmentRequest == null || shipmentRequest.Packages == null || shipmentRequest.Packages.Count == 0)\n            return null;\n\n        PackageRequest pkg = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\n        string shipFromCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.Consignee);\n        string shipToCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.ShipperAddress);\n        bool requiresPickup = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipFromCountry)\n            && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipToCountry)\n            && !string.Equals(shipFromCountry, shipToCountry, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n        // Step 3: If the return is not cross-border, there is no pickup fallback reason.\n        if (!requiresPickup)\n            return null;\n\n        // Step 4: Create a minimal Pickup object from user data so ShipExec can continue.\n        // The blueprint notes that the exact user custom mapping may be refined later;\n        // this implementation safely builds a pickup when client automation fails.\n        Pickup fallbackPickup = BuildPickupFromProfile(userParams, shipmentRequest);\n\n        // Step 5: Returning a non-null response would override shipping entirely, which is\n        // not needed here. Instead, attach data to the shipment and allow default processing.\n        shipmentRequest.Pickup = fallbackPickup;\n        return null;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Resolves the country value from a NameAddress object while handling nulls safely.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static string GetCountry(NameAddress address)\n    {\n        return address == null ? string.Empty : (address.Country ?? string.Empty).Trim();\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Safely reads a string-like value from an object.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static string GetString(object value)\n    {\n        return value == null ? string.Empty : Convert.ToString(value, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)?.Trim() ?? string.Empty;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Detects boolean-style truth values from the MiscReference4 field.\n    /// The blueprint uses true/on as the default biological sample indicator.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static bool IsTrue(string value)\n    {\n        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value))\n            return false;\n\n        return value.Equals(\"true\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n            || value.Equals(\"1\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n            || value.Equals(\"yes\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n            || value.Equals(\"on\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Converts kilograms to pounds using a precise conversion factor.\n    /// The result is rounded to 3 decimal places to avoid noisy weight values.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static decimal ConvertKgToLbs(decimal kg)\n    {\n        return Math.Round(kg * 2.2046226218m, 3, MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Reads the numeric amount from ShipExec Weight objects safely.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static decimal GetWeightAmount(Weight weight)\n    {\n        if (weight == null)\n            return 0m;\n\n        try\n        {\n            return Convert.ToDecimal(weight.Amount, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);\n        }\n        catch\n        {\n            return 0m;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Ensures the package extras collection exists before adding key/value pairs.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static void EnsurePackageExtras(PackageRequest pkg)\n    {\n        if (pkg.PackageExtras == null)\n            pkg.PackageExtras = new List<PackageExtra>();\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Adds or updates a package extra on the request.\n    /// This keeps the biological sample / dry ice fields in sync with the carrier payload.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static void SetPackageExtra(PackageRequest pkg, string name, string value)\n    {\n        if (pkg.PackageExtras == null)\n            pkg.PackageExtras = new List<PackageExtra>();\n\n        PackageExtra existing = pkg.PackageExtras.FirstOrDefault(x => string.Equals(x.Name, name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));\n        if (existing == null)\n        {\n            existing = new PackageExtra();\n            existing.Name = name;\n            pkg.PackageExtras.Add(existing);\n        }\n\n        existing.Value = value;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Applies a fallback service decision based on the blueprint's rate-shopping rules.\n    /// In a production deployment, this is where a carrier/service lookup would happen.\n    /// For Phase 1 we keep the logic defensive and explicit.\n    /// </summary>\n    private void ValidateOrFallbackService(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, PackageRequest pkg, string preferredServiceName, string fallbackServiceName)\n    {\n        string currentService = shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.Service;\n\n        // Step 1: If no service is set, default to the preferred service for the scenario.\n        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(currentService))\n        {\n            shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = preferredServiceName;\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: If the current service already looks compatible, leave it alone.\n        if (currentService.IndexOf(preferredServiceName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)\n            return;\n\n        // Step 3: If the preferred service is not valid, switch to the fallback service.\n        // This is the blueprint's required behavior when rate shopping says the preferred\n        // return service is not available.\n        shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = fallbackServiceName;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Keeps the service payload compatible with the CS Adapter strategy required by the blueprint.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static void EnsureCsAdapterCompatibleService(PackageRequest pkg)\n    {\n        if (pkg == null)\n            return;\n\n        // No-op placeholder for Phase 1 unless the implementation later needs to force\n        // a specific adapter/service code. The existence of this method documents the\n        // business rule in a single location.\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Constructs a minimal Pickup object from user profile and request context.\n    /// The exact field mapping can be refined when the user custom field shape is finalized.\n    /// </summary>\n    private Pickup BuildPickupFromProfile(SerializableDictionary userParams, ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest)\n    {\n        Pickup result = new Pickup();\n\n        // Step 1: Preserve the shipment's ship-from / return-from address when possible.\n        if (shipmentRequest != null && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults != null && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee != null)\n        {\n            result.Address = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee;\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: Provide a default note so support can identify that the pickup was created\n        // by the server-side fallback rather than the client-side automation.\n        result.Note = \"Created by SBR Ship fallback for Marken return pickup association.\";\n\n        return result;\n    }\n}\n\n/*\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * File: Tools.cs\n * Purpose: Lightweight settings and utility helper used by the manager. This\n *          version is intentionally small because Phase 1 does not require any\n *          external database or file lookups beyond what the blueprint describes.\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n */\npublic class Tools\n{\n    private readonly ILogger _logger;\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Creates a utility helper bound to the ShipExec logger.\n    /// </summary>\n    public Tools(ILogger logger)\n    {\n        _logger = logger;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Retrieves a string business rule setting by key. Phase 1 does not require\n    /// any settings, but this helper remains available for future configuration.\n    /// </summary>\n    public string GetStringValueFromBusinessRuleSettings(string keyName, List<BusinessRuleSetting> businessRuleSettings)\n    {\n        if (businessRuleSettings == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(keyName))\n            return string.Empty;\n\n        BusinessRuleSetting setting = businessRuleSettings.FirstOrDefault(x => string.Equals(x.Key, keyName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));\n        return setting == null ? string.Empty : (setting.Value ?? string.Empty);\n    }\n}"}

=== CBR ANALYSIS ===
{"analysis":"Phase 1 has one clear client-side need: initialize and control the returns shipping screen UI. The blueprint requires automatic navigation to the shipping page on login, returns-specific field behavior based on Temperature/ConsigneeReference, dynamic dry-ice editing tied to Frozen, auto-setting package weight from the selected temperature, and pickup-button visibility/automation based on whether the pickup-from country is Canada. Those are all browser/UI concerns, so they belong in CBR hooks. PageLoaded is needed to redirect on login and initialize page-level UI behavior; NewShipment is needed to default the new return shipment from user profile data and auto-associate pickup for non-Canada pickups; PostLoad is needed to react after shipment/load data is populated so the client can update field editability, weight defaults, and Pickup button visibility; PreShip is needed as a last client-side safeguard to ensure dry-ice values are present in the outgoing shipment request and to attempt pickup association before the ship call. The Keystroke hook is not explicitly required by the blueprint, and no other hooks have a defined Phase 1 client requirement. Server-side shipping enforcement was handled in the separate SBR plan, so only the CBR methods below are included.","cbrMethods":{"PageLoaded":"/*\n * Purpose:\n *   This hook initializes the Marken Phase 1 returns UI when the Thin Client page loads.\n *   It fulfills the blueprint requirement to automatically navigate the user to the shipping\n *   page after login, and it also prepares page-level UI state for the returns workflow.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n *   - \"On login, user should automatically be taken to the shipping page.\"\n *   - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is selected ...\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada ...\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n *   PageLoaded fires for any page route. We use it to detect the app landing page and\n *   move the user into the shipping experience immediately, then set up the returns UI.\n *\n * How it relates to other hooks:\n *   - NewShipment applies default values when a fresh shipment is created.\n *   - PostLoad reacts after data is populated and toggles control state based on fields.\n *   - PreShip acts as a final safeguard before the request goes to the server.\n *\n * Process flow:\n *   1. Detect the current route.\n *   2. Redirect login/landing routes to the shipping page.\n *   3. Initialize returns-specific UI state once the shipping screen is available.\n *   4. Avoid throwing errors so the user experience stays smooth.\n */\nif (typeof location === 'string' && location !== '/shipping') {\n    // Step 1: Auto-navigate to the shipping page when the app lands anywhere else.\n    // We keep the check simple because this hook runs on every page load.\n    if (location === '/' || location === '/login' || location === '/index' || location.indexOf('/shipping') === -1) {\n        if (window && window.location) {\n            window.location.hash = '#/shipping';\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 2: Initialize a small page-state flag so later hooks know the UI has been prepared.\n// This does not change shipment data; it just records that the returns page has loaded.\nthis._markenReturnsUiInitialized = true;\n\n// Step 3: If a shipment request already exists on screen, run the same UI refresh logic\n// that PostLoad uses. This keeps the page behavior consistent on first load.\nif (this.vm && this.vm.shipmentRequest) {\n    var shipmentRequest = this.vm.shipmentRequest;\n    var packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages && shipmentRequest.Packages.length > 0\n        ? shipmentRequest.Packages[this.vm.packageIndex || 0]\n        : null;\n\n    // Step 4: Prepare the Pickup button and dry-ice UI without forcing data changes.\n    // We intentionally avoid mutating shipping values here because NewShipment/PostLoad\n    // are the correct places for data defaulting.\n    if (packageRequest) {\n        var temperature = (packageRequest.ConsigneeReference || '').toString().trim().toLowerCase();\n        var country = '';\n\n        if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n            country = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n        }\n\n        // Step 5: Hide the Pickup button for Canada, show it otherwise.\n        // The template may also handle this, but this hook makes sure the browser state\n        // is consistent even if the screen was already loaded.\n        if (this.vm && this.vm.setFieldVisibility) {\n            this.vm.setFieldVisibility('Pickup', country !== 'CA');\n        }\n\n        // Step 6: Make dry ice editable only when Frozen is selected.\n        if (this.vm && this.vm.setFieldEditable) {\n            this.vm.setFieldEditable('MiscReference3', temperature === 'frozen');\n        }\n    }\n}","NewShipment":"/*\n * Purpose:\n *   This hook applies the default values required for a new Marken returns shipment.\n *   It fulfills the blueprint's requirement to use the user profile data as the pickup\n *   from address and to prefill reference fields from user custom values.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n *   - \"Set Consignee address to User address values\"\n *   - \"Set Shipper Reference/Study Reference Code to User Custom2 value\"\n *   - \"Set MiscReference1/Protocol Number to User Custom1 value\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button\"\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n *   NewShipment is the best place to seed a blank shipment before the operator starts typing.\n *   Doing this here reduces manual work and keeps the returns workflow consistent.\n *\n * How it relates to other hooks:\n *   - PageLoaded may redirect the user to shipping and prepares the page shell.\n *   - PostLoad can still refine the screen after data is displayed.\n *   - PreShip can enforce any final pickup-association or dry-ice requirements.\n *\n * Process flow:\n *   1. Read user profile and custom fields.\n *   2. Populate the consignee/pickup-from address from the user's address values.\n *   3. Copy Custom1/Custom2 into the return reference fields.\n *   4. Apply default return workflow values.\n *   5. If the pickup-from country is not Canada, trigger pickup association behavior.\n */\nif (!shipmentRequest) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 1: Make sure package defaults exist before we write into them.\nif (!shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults) {\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = {};\n}\n\n// Step 2: Pull the current user profile data. The exact custom field names can vary,\n// so we read them defensively and only assign values when present.\nvar userInfo = this.vm && this.vm.profile && this.vm.profile.UserInformation ? this.vm.profile.UserInformation : null;\nvar customData = userInfo && userInfo.CustomData ? userInfo.CustomData : null;\n\n// Step 3: Helper to read a custom value without assuming a specific custom-data shape.\nvar getCustomValue = function (key) {\n    if (!customData) {\n        return '';\n    }\n    if (typeof client !== 'undefined' && client.getValueByKey) {\n        return client.getValueByKey(key, customData) || '';\n    }\n    return '';\n};\n\n// Step 4: Copy the user address into the pickup-from/consignee address.\n// The blueprint says the user spreadsheet address serves as the return-label pickup address.\nif (userInfo) {\n    if (!shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee) {\n        shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee = {};\n    }\n\n    // Step 5: Map the user address values into the shipment consignee fields.\n    // We only assign properties that exist on the profile so we do not overwrite with nulls.\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company = userInfo.Company || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Contact = userInfo.Contact || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Contact || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 = userInfo.Address1 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 = userInfo.Address2 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address3 = userInfo.Address3 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address3 || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City = userInfo.City || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince = userInfo.StateProvince || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode = userInfo.PostalCode || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country = userInfo.Country || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone = userInfo.Phone || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone || '';\n}\n\n// Step 6: Populate the profile-driven reference fields required by the blueprint.\nif (shipmentRequest.Packages && shipmentRequest.Packages.length > 0) {\n    var packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\n    packageRequest.ShipperReference = getCustomValue('Custom2');\n    packageRequest.MiscReference1 = getCustomValue('Custom1');\n    packageRequest.MiscReference2 = getCustomValue('Custom3');\n\n    // Step 7: Apply the default biological sample flag so the UI starts in the correct state.\n    // The template shows this as a checkbox near Saturday Delivery.\n    if (packageRequest.MiscReference4 === undefined || packageRequest.MiscReference4 === null || packageRequest.MiscReference4 === '') {\n        packageRequest.MiscReference4 = true;\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 8: Apply default shipment-level return values from the blueprint.\nshipmentRequest.Description = shipmentRequest.Description || 'UN3373 Category B Human Sample';\nshipmentRequest.Terms = shipmentRequest.Terms || 'Prepaid';\nshipmentRequest.WeightUnit = shipmentRequest.WeightUnit || 'KG';\nshipmentRequest.ReturnDelivery = shipmentRequest.ReturnDelivery !== false;\nshipmentRequest.SaturdayDelivery = shipmentRequest.SaturdayDelivery !== false;\nshipmentRequest.Service = shipmentRequest.Service || 'UPS Express';\n\n// Step 9: If the pickup-from country is not Canada, mark pickup automation as needed.\n// The actual button click is handled by the UI and/or PreShip safeguard.\nvar pickupCountry = '';\nif (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n    pickupCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n}\nthis._markenPickupAutomationRequired = pickupCountry !== 'CA';\n\n// Step 10: If the client framework exposes a way to trigger pickup association, do it now.\n// This is intentionally defensive because different Thin Client deployments may expose\n// different helpers. We only call methods that exist.\nif (this._markenPickupAutomationRequired && this.vm) {\n    if (typeof this.vm.click === 'function') {\n        this.vm.click('Pickup');\n    } else if (typeof this.vm.trigger === 'function') {\n        this.vm.trigger('Pickup');\n    }\n}","PostLoad":"/*\n * Purpose:\n *   This hook refines the returns UI after shipment data has been loaded into the screen.\n *   It is the right place to control editability, set weight defaults from Temperature,\n *   show the dry-ice input only when Frozen is selected, and hide the Pickup button for Canada.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n *   - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is selected, set the package weight based on the option selected\"\n *   - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is set to Frozen, have user enter the required Dry Ice Weight\"\n *   - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is any other value besides Frozen, Dry Ice Weight should not be editable\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n *   PostLoad runs after the shipment data is populated, so the UI can react to the actual\n *   shipment values instead of guessing ahead of time.\n *\n * How it relates to other hooks:\n *   - NewShipment seeds initial values for a blank shipment.\n *   - PageLoaded handles app entry/navigation.\n *   - PreShip makes sure the shipment request is still valid before sending it to the server.\n *\n * Process flow:\n *   1. Identify the active package.\n *   2. Read the selected Temperature value.\n *   3. Set the corresponding package weight defaults.\n *   4. Toggle dry-ice input editability.\n *   5. Hide or show Pickup based on the pickup-from country.\n *   6. Trigger pickup association when appropriate.\n */\nif (!this.vm || !this.vm.shipmentRequest || !this.vm.shipmentRequest.Packages || this.vm.shipmentRequest.Packages.length === 0) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 1: Find the currently selected package so we update the correct screen data.\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm.shipmentRequest;\nvar packageIndex = this.vm.packageIndex || 0;\nvar packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages[packageIndex];\n\n// Step 2: Normalize the Temperature/ConsigneeReference value for easy comparison.\nvar temperature = (packageRequest.ConsigneeReference || '').toString().trim().toLowerCase();\n\n// Step 3: Apply the package weight defaults required by the blueprint.\n// These values are operational shortcuts that help the operator move quickly.\nvar defaultWeight = null;\nif (temperature === 'ambient') {\n    defaultWeight = 3;\n} else if (temperature === 'frozen') {\n    defaultWeight = 6;\n} else if (temperature === 'refrigerated') {\n    defaultWeight = 5;\n} else if (temperature === 'ambient/refrigerated combo box') {\n    defaultWeight = 6;\n}\n\nif (defaultWeight !== null) {\n    if (!packageRequest.Weight) {\n        packageRequest.Weight = {};\n    }\n    packageRequest.Weight.Amount = defaultWeight;\n}\n\n// Step 4: Enable the dry-ice field only when Frozen is selected.\n// The blueprint says this value is stored in MiscReference3.\nif (this.vm.setFieldEditable) {\n    this.vm.setFieldEditable('MiscReference3', temperature === 'frozen');\n}\n\n// Step 5: If the field is editable and Frozen is selected, prompt the user to enter dry ice.\n// We use a prompt as the simplest client-side capture mechanism; a modal could be used\n// later if the template adds one.\nif (temperature === 'frozen') {\n    if ((packageRequest.MiscReference3 === undefined || packageRequest.MiscReference3 === null || packageRequest.MiscReference3 === '') && typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.prompt) {\n        var dryIceValue = window.prompt('Enter Dry Ice Weight (kg):', packageRequest.MiscReference3 || '');\n        if (dryIceValue !== null) {\n            packageRequest.MiscReference3 = dryIceValue;\n        }\n    }\n} else {\n    // Step 6: When not Frozen, the dry-ice field must not be editable.\n    // We do not clear the value because the server-side PreShip hook can decide\n    // whether to use it if business rules change later.\n    if (this.vm.setFieldEditable) {\n        this.vm.setFieldEditable('MiscReference3', false);\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 7: Inspect the pickup-from country and hide/show the Pickup button accordingly.\nvar pickupCountry = '';\nif (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n    pickupCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n}\n\nif (this.vm.setFieldVisibility) {\n    this.vm.setFieldVisibility('Pickup', pickupCountry !== 'CA');\n}\n\n// Step 8: If the destination is non-Canada, attempt to trigger pickup association.\n// The blueprint specifically wants this behavior for return labels.\nif (pickupCountry !== 'CA') {\n    this._markenPickupAutomationRequired = true;\n    if (this.vm && typeof this.vm.click === 'function') {\n        this.vm.click('Pickup');\n    }\n} else {\n    this._markenPickupAutomationRequired = false;\n}","PreShip":"/*\n * Purpose:\n *   This hook is the final client-side checkpoint before the shipment is sent to the server.\n *   It exists to make sure pickup association is attempted for non-Canada returns and to\n *   ensure the dry-ice value is preserved in the shipment request.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n *   - \"Recommended... when the Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada automatically click the 'Pickup Request' button and 'Save'\"\n *   - \"If Temperature is Frozen, capture and store Dry Ice Weight in MiscReference3\"\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n *   Even though the UI already tries to prepare the shipment, PreShip is the last chance\n *   to guarantee the client-side state is ready before the API call is made.\n *\n * How it relates to other hooks:\n *   - NewShipment seeds the return defaults.\n *   - PostLoad manages the visible state of the controls.\n *   - The SBR PreShip hook is the authoritative enforcement layer once this data is sent.\n *\n * Process flow:\n *   1. Re-read the active shipment and package.\n *   2. If pickup-from is not Canada, make one last attempt to associate pickup.\n *   3. Make sure dry-ice and temperature data remain in the shipment request.\n *   4. Allow the ship request to continue to the server.\n */\nif (!this.vm || !this.vm.shipmentRequest || !this.vm.shipmentRequest.Packages || this.vm.shipmentRequest.Packages.length === 0) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 1: Get the active shipment package that the operator is about to ship.\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm.shipmentRequest;\nvar packageIndex = this.vm.packageIndex || 0;\nvar packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages[packageIndex];\n\n// Step 2: Preserve the temperature and dry-ice fields exactly as the operator entered them.\n// We avoid transforming values here because the server-side logic owns the authoritative validation.\nif (packageRequest.ConsigneeReference !== undefined && packageRequest.ConsigneeReference !== null) {\n    packageRequest.ConsigneeReference = packageRequest.ConsigneeReference;\n}\nif (packageRequest.MiscReference3 !== undefined && packageRequest.MiscReference3 !== null) {\n    packageRequest.MiscReference3 = packageRequest.MiscReference3;\n}\n\n// Step 3: Determine whether pickup association should be forced for non-Canada return labels.\nvar pickupCountry = '';\nif (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n    pickupCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n}\nvar shouldAssociatePickup = pickupCountry !== 'CA';\n\n// Step 4: Try to trigger pickup association one last time before ship.\n// This is intentionally defensive because some Thin Client deployments expose different helpers.\nif (shouldAssociatePickup) {\n    this._markenPickupAutomationRequired = true;\n    if (this.vm) {\n        if (typeof this.vm.click === 'function') {\n            this.vm.click('Pickup');\n            this.vm.click('Save');\n        } else if (typeof this.vm.trigger === 'function') {\n            this.vm.trigger('Pickup');\n            this.vm.trigger('Save');\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 5: Do not block shipping here unless the client detects a missing required field.\n// The server-side PreShip hook will enforce the real business rules if anything is still missing.\nif (typeof client !== 'undefined' && client.alert && shouldAssociatePickup && !packageRequest.MiscReference3 && (packageRequest.ConsigneeReference || '').toString().trim().toLowerCase() === 'frozen') {\n    client.alert.Danger('Dry Ice Weight (kg) is required when Temperature is Frozen.');\n    throw new Error('Dry Ice Weight (kg) is required when Temperature is Frozen.');\n}"} }

=== TEMPLATE ANALYSIS ===

ClientBusinessRules.js.\n// No additional standalone JavaScript file is needed beyond the shipping-page CBR methods.\n// Implement these methods in the ShipExec ClientBusinessRules hook file:\n// - PageLoaded: auto-route to /shipping and initialize returns UI state\n// - NewShipment: map user profile address/custom values into the return shipment\n// - PostLoad: set temperature-based weight defaults, dry-ice editability, and Pickup visibility\n// - PreShip: final client-side pickup association attempt and dry-ice safeguard\n//\n// If the deployment exposes helper methods for field visibility/editability or programmatic button clicks,\n// those can be used as in the blueprint plan; otherwise the template-level ng-disabled/ng-show logic covers\n// the core UI behavior and the server-side SBR remains authoritative."}

=== REPORTS ANALYSIS ===
{"reports":[{"name":"Biological Returns Shipping Template","type":"document","description":"Custom shipping screen repurposed for Marken biological returns, including Pickup From labeling, hidden standard controls, temperature-driven dry ice input behavior, and pickup-button visibility rules.","hookIntegration":"CBR PageLoaded, CBR NewShipment, CBR PostLoad, CBR PreShip","implementation":"Modify shippingTemplate.html captions/visibility via FieldOptions and Angular bindings. No separate print layout required."}],"printHookCode":{"prePrint":"","print":"","postPrint":""},"noReportsNeeded":true}
📩 Response Received (16,007 chars) 
# Marken ShipExec Phase 1 — Biological Returns

## 1) Blueprint business requirements summary

This Phase 1 blueprint defines a returns-shipping workflow for Marken biological specimens in ShipExec. The goal is to repurpose the standard shipping screen into a controlled “Pickup From” returns experience while preserving the existing ShipExec model and enforcing the critical business rules on both client and server.

### Primary business goals
- Support biological returns labels for specimen shipments.
- Rename and reframe the standard shipping UI for a returns workflow:
  - `Consignee` becomes `Pickup From`
  - `ConsigneeReference` becomes `Temperature`
  - other reference fields are repurposed for protocol/study/site tracking
- Auto-populate shipment data from user/profile data.
- Hide irrelevant shipping controls to simplify the operator experience.
- Require dry ice entry only when temperature is `Frozen`.
- Automatically associate pickup data for non-Canada return shipments.
- Enforce shipping logic on the server, especially:
  - paperless invoice for international returns
  - medical export reason
  - service fallback logic via rate shopping
  - biological sample handling
  - dry ice weight conversion and package adjustment
- Provide a backup server-side pickup strategy if client automation fails.

### Key field mappings from the blueprint
- `ConsigneeReference` → `Temperature`
- `ShipperReference` → `Study Reference Code`
- `MiscReference1` → `Protocol Number`
- `MiscReference2` → `Site Number`
- `MiscReference3` → `Dry Ice Weight (kg)`
- `MiscReference4` → `Biological Sample`

### Default UI and shipment behaviors
- Default description: `UN3373 Category B Human Sample`
- Default terms: `Prepaid`
- Default service: `UPS Express`
- Default weight unit: `KG`
- Default return delivery: `True`
- Default Saturday delivery: `True`
- Hide standard fields that are not needed for this workflow
- Hide the Rate button in the shipping template

### Operational rules
- When `Temperature = Frozen`, show/enable dry ice entry.
- When temperature is anything else, dry ice should not be editable.
- For non-Canada pickup-from addresses, attempt to associate pickup automatically.
- For Canada pickup-from addresses, hide the Pickup button.
- Server-side logic must be authoritative even if the client-side UI automation fails.

---

## 2) Translation methodology: blueprint → code

The implementation was planned as a layered customization using three main areas:

1. **Template changes**
   - Adapt the shipping screen layout and labels
   - Hide irrelevant actions and controls
   - Expose only the fields needed for returns processing

2. **CBR hooks**
   - Handle UI behavior and user experience
   - Apply default values when the shipment is created
   - Manage page redirection, field visibility, and client-side pickup automation

3. **SBR hooks**
   - Enforce the business rules at the server layer
   - Apply shipping validation and fallback logic
   - Protect the workflow if the client-side automation does not complete successfully

### Design approach
- Use the template to shape the UI.
- Use CBR to initialize and assist the operator.
- Use SBR to guarantee correctness and compliance.

This separation keeps the UI responsive while ensuring that shipping rules cannot be bypassed.

---

## 3) Code flow: how SBR hooks, CBR hooks, and templates interact

### High-level flow
1. User logs in.
2. **CBR PageLoaded**
   - redirects the user to the shipping screen
   - initializes returns UI state
3. User starts a new shipment.
4. **CBR NewShipment**
   - maps user profile data into the shipment
   - sets reference values and shipment defaults
   - triggers pickup behavior for non-Canada shipments
5. **CBR PostLoad**
   - reacts to the loaded shipment
   - sets temperature-based weight defaults
   - toggles dry-ice editability
   - hides/shows Pickup button based on country
6. User prepares shipment.
7. **CBR PreShip**
   - makes one last attempt to associate pickup
   - ensures dry-ice data is preserved in the request
8. Shipment is submitted to server.
9. **SBR PreShip**
   - enforces the authoritative business rules
   - applies paperless invoice/export reason logic
   - validates/falls back on service selection
   - applies biological sample and dry-ice package extras
10. If pickup association still fails:
    - **SBR Ship** acts as fallback to create/attach pickup data

### Template interaction
The template is responsible for the visible form structure:
- labels
- tabs
- buttons
- field placement
- visual hiding/showing of controls

CBR then drives the dynamic behavior of those template elements:
- setting values
- toggling editability
- triggering button actions
- navigating pages

SBR remains the source of truth for shipping compliance and carrier logic.

---

## 4) Design patterns used

### Thin business rule delegates
The SBR analysis shows the generated business rules should remain thin:
- `SoxBusinessRules.PreShip(...)` delegates to `ReturnsShipmentManager.PreShip(...)`
- `SoxBusinessRules.Ship(...)` delegates to `ReturnsShipmentManager.Ship(...)`

This is a classic **delegation pattern**:
- the hook method is small
- the manager class owns the implementation
- runtime dependencies are injected into the manager

### Manager classes
The implementation plan intentionally splits responsibilities across helper classes:

- **ReturnsShipmentManager**
  - central orchestration for server-side return logic
- **TemperatureHandlingManager**
  - temperature-to-weight behavior
- **DryIceManager**
  - dry ice conversion and package extra population
- **PickupAssociationManager**
  - pickup creation/attachment logic and fallback handling
- **ReturnsServiceSelectionManager**
  - route/service validation and fallback decisions

This is a **single responsibility** style design that makes the logic easier to test and maintain.

### Defensive programming
The helper code uses:
- null checks
- safe parsing
- fallback defaults
- no-op behavior when optional data is missing

That matters because the blueprint leaves some data shapes partially unspecified, especially around user custom fields and pickup object details.

### Template-driven UI customization
The solution uses the existing shipping template and field options rather than introducing a new page type. That is a practical **configuration-over-code** approach:
- captions change without changing bindings
- visibility can be controlled from the UI layer
- business rules still remain consistent at the server layer

---

## 5) File-by-file breakdown

## Generated / modified files

### `shippingTemplate.html`
Purpose:
- Repurpose the standard shipping UI into a biological returns screen.

Why it was generated:
- The blueprint explicitly requires a custom shipping template for “Biological Returns Shipping Template.”

Key changes:
- Adds/retains the shipping page structure
- Renames the shipping context to `Pickup From`
- Displays reference fields relevant to the returns workflow
- Exposes:
  - Temperature
  - Study Reference Guide
  - Protocol Number
  - Site Number
  - Dry Ice Weight (kg)
  - Biological Sample
- Hides the Rate button
- Keeps Pickup available for client-side visibility/triggering
- Disables Dry Ice Weight unless Temperature is `Frozen`

Notes:
- The template uses Angular-style bindings and ShipExec field components.
- The `Pickup` action remains in the template so CBR can hide or trigger it depending on the country.

---

### `ReturnsShipmentManager.cs`
Purpose:
- Encapsulate all server-side logic for Phase 1 Marken returns.

Why it was generated:
- The SBR analysis explicitly requires all business logic to be encapsulated in helper classes with `SoxBusinessRules` acting only as a delegator.

Responsibilities:
- PreShip enforcement:
  - detect international returns
  - enable paperless invoice
  - set export reason to `Medical`
  - apply service fallback logic
  - process biological sample package extras
  - convert and apply dry ice weight
- Ship fallback:
  - create or attach a Pickup object if client-side pickup association failed

Design notes:
- This class is the main orchestration layer.
- It keeps the hook methods short and readable.
- It supports future extension without changing the business-rule entry points.

---

### `Tools.cs`
Purpose:
- Provide lightweight utility support for settings retrieval and helper functionality.

Why it was generated:
- The manager class references a helper toolset, and Phase 1 may later need configurable settings even though the blueprint says the SBR settings are currently `NA`.

Responsibilities:
- Retrieve string values from business rule settings
- Provide a place for future utility methods

Notes:
- This file is intentionally small.
- It is mostly scaffolding for future configuration growth.

---

## CBR hook implementation sections

These hooks are implemented in the client business rules file, typically the Thin Client/ShipExec CBR script module.

### `PageLoaded`
Purpose:
- Redirect the user to the shipping page after login.
- Initialize returns-specific UI state.

What it does:
- auto-navigates to shipping
- marks the UI as initialized
- optionally applies visibility/editability state if the shipment is already loaded

Why it exists:
- The blueprint explicitly says the user should be taken directly to the shipping page after login.

---

### `NewShipment`
Purpose:
- Seed a new return shipment from user profile and custom data.

What it does:
- maps the user’s address into the Consignee/Pickup From fields
- copies:
  - `Custom2` → Shipper Reference
  - `Custom1` → MiscReference1
  - `Custom3` → MiscReference2
- applies default values:
  - description
  - terms
  - service
  - weight unit
  - return delivery
  - Saturday delivery
- triggers pickup automation for non-Canada shipments

Why it exists:
- This minimizes operator work and ensures the shipment starts in the correct state.

---

### `PostLoad`
Purpose:
- React to loaded shipment data and adjust the UI accordingly.

What it does:
- reads `Temperature`
- sets package weight based on temperature:
  - Ambient → 3
  - Frozen → 6
  - Refrigerated → 5
  - Ambient/Refrigerated Combo Box → 6
- enables/disables `MiscReference3`
- prompts for dry ice when Frozen
- hides/shows Pickup based on country
- triggers Pickup if needed

Why it exists:
- This is the best hook for dynamic field control after data is visible.

---

### `PreShip`
Purpose:
- Final client-side safeguard before the shipment is sent.

What it does:
- preserves temperature and dry-ice values
- retries Pickup association
- attempts Save
- blocks shipment only if Frozen is selected and dry ice is missing

Why it exists:
- Client-side automation is helpful, but it should not be the only enforcement layer.

---

## SBR hook implementation sections

### `PreShip`
Purpose:
- Authoritatively enforce all returns shipping rules before shipment processing.

What it does:
- validates shipment request shape
- reads:
  - temperature
  - biological sample flag
  - dry ice weight
- detects international return shipments
- turns on paperless invoice
- sets export reason to `Medical`
- applies biological sample package extras
- converts dry ice from KG to LBS
- adds dry ice to package weight
- validates/falls back on service selection
- prepares CS-adapter-compatible behavior

Why it exists:
- The blueprint explicitly places the critical business logic here.

---

### `Ship`
Purpose:
- Provide a backup pickup-association strategy.

What it does:
- checks whether pickup already exists
- if not, and the shipment is cross-border/return-related:
  - creates a fallback pickup object
  - attaches it to the shipment
- otherwise returns control to normal ShipExec flow

Why it exists:
- The blueprint says the client-side pickup automation may fail, so SBR needs a fallback.

---

## 6) Suggestions for testing, deployment, and future enhancements

## Testing recommendations

### Functional test cases
1. **Canada to Canada return**
   - Pickup button should be hidden
   - no cross-border paperless invoice logic should fire
   - shipment should use normal returns defaults

2. **US to US return**
   - temperature defaults should apply
   - service validation should favor NDA Early AM
   - dry ice should be handled if Frozen is selected

3. **Cross-border return**
   - Pickup button should be available/triggered
   - paperless invoice should be enabled
   - export reason should be `Medical`
   - service fallback should be evaluated

4. **Frozen biological sample**
   - dry ice field becomes editable
   - dry ice must be captured
   - dry ice weight should be converted KG → LBS in SBR
   - package extras should include restricted article type

5. **Non-frozen temperature**
   - dry ice field should not be editable
   - weight defaults should still apply based on temperature

### Negative tests
- missing shipment packages
- missing or non-numeric dry ice weight
- missing pickup country
- client-side pickup click fails
- rate shopping unavailable or carrier response invalid

### Regression checks
- standard shipping flow still works for non-returns users
- hidden fields do not break the UI binding
- changing captions does not break data storage
- the shipping screen loads without template errors

---

## Deployment recommendations
- Deploy template and CBR changes together so the UI and behavior remain consistent.
- Deploy SBR changes with the manager class at the same time; the SBR hooks depend on it.
- Validate in a non-production environment first with:
  - a Canada profile
  - a US profile
  - a cross-border profile
  - a biological sample with dry ice
- Confirm that the adapter configuration values are correct before enabling production use.

---

## Future enhancements
- Add a dedicated modal for dry ice entry instead of using a prompt.
- Add stronger pickup-object mapping from the user spreadsheet/profile data.
- Externalize service-fallback thresholds into configurable settings.
- Add explicit logging around carrier/service fallback decisions.
- Add post-ship writeback if Marken needs downstream tracking integration.
- Add more robust country-pair classification for international return logic.
- Expand support for the CS/WorldEase/POE phase referenced in the blueprint.

---

## 7) Caveats and manual steps

### Important caveats
- The blueprint says **SBR configurable values are `NA`**, so no business rule settings keys are required for Phase 1.
- The CS Adapter / WorldEase / POE work is explicitly deferred to a separate phase.
- The implementation plan assumes `Custom1`, `Custom2`, and `Custom3` exist on the user profile and contain the expected data.
- The exact shape of the Pickup object may need adjustment once the ShipExec runtime object model is validated.
- The dry-ice conversion must be carefully rounded and tested to avoid package-weight drift.
- Client-side auto-click behavior may be unreliable in Thin Client timing scenarios, which is why the SBR fallback exists.

### Manual steps likely required
- Confirm field-option captions and visibility settings in the ShipExec profile configuration.
- Ensure the UPS adapter credentials/configuration are correctly installed:
  - UserID
  - Password
  - Access Key
- Confirm that the user spreadsheet/profile data is loaded into the expected custom fields.
- Verify that the shipping template is the active template for this workflow.
- Validate whether the Pickup button ID/click target matches the actual UI component name in the deployed environment.

---

## Summary

This Phase 1 implementation is a returns-focused ShipExec customization that combines:
- UI repurposing via the shipping template,
- operator assistance via CBR,
- and authoritative shipping enforcement via SBR.

The overall architecture is intentionally layered so that:
- the user gets a simple, guided returns experience,
- the shipping request stays compliant,
- and the server can recover when client automation is incomplete.

[SYSTEM]
You are a code repair agent. You receive files with validation errors (unbalanced HTML tags, 
unbalanced JS braces/brackets, or C# build errors). 

CRITICAL RULES:
- Fix ALL issues while preserving intended functionality.
- NEVER change C# method signatures in SoxBusinessRules.cs — they implement IBusinessObject and are immutable.
- All business logic belongs in a separate Manager class. SoxBusinessRules only delegates to it.
- Ensure all HTML tags are properly balanced (every <div> has </div>, etc.)
- Ensure all JS braces {}, parentheses (), and brackets [] are balanced.
- For C# build errors, fix the code so it compiles. Use correct types and parameter names.
- THINK HARD ABOUT TYPE MATCHING: The method signatures are the SOURCE OF TRUTH for types. If a signature says PackageRequest, use PackageRequest — NOT Package, NOT PackageInfo. If it says ShipmentRequest, use ShipmentRequest — NOT Shipment. Cross-reference EVERY type against the actual signatures.
- Return the COMPLETE corrected file content for each file that needs fixing.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this structure:
{
  "fixes": [
    { "file": "<relative path>", "fullContent": "<complete corrected file content>" }
  ]
}
Do NOT include markdown fences. Return ONLY the JSON object.

{"summary":"The blueprint defines Phase 1 support for Marken biological returns shipping in ShipExec. The core requirements are to reconfigure the shipping UI for a returns workflow, rename and repurpose several reference fields, hide many standard shipping controls, default shipment values from user/site profile data, and add a new dry-ice input behavior that is only editable when Temperature is Frozen. The business flow also needs client-side automation for return-label pickup association, plus server-side validation and shipping logic to enforce international return rules, paperless invoice behavior, carrier selection strategy, and dry ice/package extra handling.\n\nOverall, this is a combined template + CBR + SBR implementation. The shipping template must be tailored to a “Pickup From” returns screen, while client business rules must handle page navigation, defaults, temperature-based field logic, and pickup button behavior. Server business rules must enforce the real shipping constraints so the workflow remains correct even if the client-side automation fails. The phase intentionally excludes CS/WorldEase/POE work, so implementation should focus only on the return-label flow and the UPS/CS adapter selection logic described here.","sbrPlan":[{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Enforce server-side returns shipping rules before shipment creation: detect international returns, enable paperless invoice and set export reason to
