when the shipping page is reached, initialize returns-specific UI state such as hiding/showing the Pickup button based on the consignee/pickup country and preparing temperature-related field behavior."},{"method":"NewShipment","purpose":"Default a new return shipment using user/site profile data: set the Consignee/Pickup From address from the user address data, prefill Shipper Reference from Custom2, MiscReference1 from Custom1, default shipper/service/terms/weight unit values, and auto-trigger pickup association when the address is non-Canada."},{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Perform client-side preparation immediately before shipping: if Pickup From is non-Canada, attempt to associate a pickup and save it before ship, and ensure any temperature/dry-ice values are present in the shipment request so the server receives them."},{"method":"PostLoad","purpose":"When a load or scan occurs, apply returns-specific UI updates such as setting package weight from Temperature selection, opening the dry-ice input prompt when Frozen is selected, preventing edits when Temperature is not Frozen, and hiding or disabling the Pickup button when the address is Canadian."},{"method":"Keystroke","purpose":"Support fast operator interaction on the shipping screen, potentially to react to temperature changes or force focus/submit behavior in the returns workflow without requiring manual mouse actions."},{"method":"PreBuildShipment","purpose":"As a safeguard, ensure the shipment object assembled from the UI contains the correct user-entered dry-ice value and any return-specific defaults before the object is posted to the server."}],"cbrPlan":[{"method":"PageLoaded","purpose":"Auto-navigate to the shipping page on login, and when the shipping page is reached, initialize returns-specific UI state such as hiding/showing the Pickup button based on the consignee/pickup country and preparing temperature-related field behavior."},{"method":"NewShipment","purpose":"Default a new return shipment using user/site profile data: set the Consignee/Pickup From address from the user address data, prefill Shipper Reference from Custom2, MiscReference1 from Custom1, default shipper/service/terms/weight unit values, and auto-trigger pickup association when the address is non-Canada."},{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Perform client-side preparation immediately before shipping: if Pickup From is non-Canada, attempt to associate a pickup and save it before ship, and ensure any temperature/dry-ice values are present in the shipment request so the server receives them."},{"method":"PostLoad","purpose":"When a load or scan occurs, apply returns-specific UI updates such as setting package weight from Temperature selection, opening the dry-ice input prompt when Frozen is selected, preventing edits when Temperature is not Frozen, and hiding or disabling the Pickup button when the address is Canadian."},{"method":"Keystroke","purpose":"Support fast operator interaction on the shipping screen, potentially to react to temperature changes or force focus/submit behavior in the returns workflow without requiring manual mouse actions."},{"method":"PreBuildShipment","purpose":"As a safeguard, ensure the shipment object assembled from the UI contains the correct user-entered dry-ice value and any return-specific defaults before the object is posted to the server."}],"templatePlan":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","changes":"Rename the Consignee tab to Pickup From via FieldOptions/caption overrides and ensure the shipping layout supports the returns workflow. Hide the Rate button per blueprint, hide the Pickup button when the destination country is Canada, and expose a dry-ice field or modal trigger tied to MiscReference3 only when Temperature is Frozen. Ensure the relevant fields (ConsigneeReference, ShipperReference, MiscReference1-4) are present and mapped to the renamed labels."},{"file":"rateResultModal.html","changes":"No structural change is required unless the template exposes service-selection actions that need to be visually suppressed for this workflow. If the product requires hiding rate selection entirely for the shipping screen, ensure any rate-related entry points from the shipping template do not navigate here."},{"file":"closeManifestTemplate.html","changes":"No blueprint-specific change identified for Phase 1."},{"file":"transmitTemplate.html","changes":"No blueprint-specific change identified for Phase 1."},{"file":"groupmanagerTemplate.html","changes":"No blueprint-specific change identified for Phase 1."}],"managerClasses":[{"name":"ReturnsShipmentManager","responsibility":"Encapsulate returns-specific shipment initialization, pickup association preparation, and profile/user data mapping for NewShipment and PreShip flows."},{"name":"TemperatureHandlingManager","responsibility":"Map ConsigneeReference/Temperature values to package weight defaults, control dry-ice editability, and translate frozen shipment inputs into shipment request fields."},{"name":"DryIceManager","responsibility":"Validate and convert dry-ice weight from MiscReference3 in KG to package-level values in LBS, and populate dry-ice purpose/regulation/package extras for the SBR PreShip hook."},{"name":"PickupAssociationManager","responsibility":"Determine whether Pickup must be associated, manage client-side/server-side fallback strategies, and prepare or create Pickup objects when the pickup button automation fails."},{"name":"ReturnsServiceSelectionManager","responsibility":"Apply the service validation and fallback rules for domestic/international returns, including UPS/CS adapter selection and Saturday-delivery fallback logic."}],"risks":["Client-side auto-clicking Save/Pickup may be unreliable in Thin Client timing, so the server-side backup pickup strategy in SBR Ship should be implemented defensively.","The blueprint mixes UI field renaming with underlying ShipExec property names; captions can change without breaking bindings, but property names and data mapping must remain consistent.","Dry-ice weight conversion from KG to LBS must be precise and should account for numeric parsing/rounding so package weight is not corrupted.","The international return rules rely on determining country pairs correctly; misclassification could incorrectly enable paperless invoice or the wrong service fallback.","Service validation by rate shopping can be slow or fail if carrier APIs are unavailable; the implementation should handle exceptions and choose safe fallback behavior.","Hidden fields in profile/FieldOptions may conflict with template-level visibility changes if both are applied inconsistently.","The blueprint does not define exact user-custom field shapes beyond Custom1/2/3 and address data, so mapping assumptions need confirmation before coding."],"implementationOrder":["Confirm/record the exact FieldOptions and profile defaults required for the returns workflow, including captions, hidden fields, and default values.","Update shippingTemplate.html and profile-driven captions/visibility so the UI reflects the Pickup From returns flow and hides disallowed controls like Rate and Pickup for Canada.","Implement CBR PageLoaded and NewShipment first to establish the UI defaults, address mapping, and country-based Pickup button behavior.","Implement CBR PreShip and any helper methods for attempting pickup association and passing dry-ice/user data forward.","Implement SBR PreShip to enforce the authoritative shipping rules: paperless invoice, export reason, adapter/service selection, dry-ice translation, and package extra setup.","Implement SBR Ship as the fallback pickup-association strategy if client automation does not reliably attach the Pickup object.","Add any supporting manager/helper classes for returns mapping, dry ice, service selection, and pickup handling, then wire them into SoxBusinessRules.","Perform end-to-end testing for Canada domestic returns, US/CA cross-border returns, and frozen dry-ice returns to verify UI behavior, field mapping, and server-side enforcement."]}

=== STEP 2: SBR ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint requires only server-side enforcement and fallback logic in SBR for Phase 1. The authoritative shipping logic belongs in SBR PreShip because it must: detect international return labels, enable paperless invoice and set export reason to Medical, choose between CS and UPS service strategies, validate domestic/international service fallbacks by rate shopping, and apply biological sample/dry-ice package extras. The SBR Ship hook is only needed as a backup when the client-side pickup association fails; it should create or attach a Pickup object from user custom/profile data, but otherwise allow the default ship flow. PostShip, PreRate, and PostRate are not explicitly required by the blueprint as server hooks because the blueprint’s rate-shopping validation is part of the PreShip enforcement step, and there is no post-ship integration/writeback requirement defined for Phase 1. No BusinessRuleSettings keys are required because the blueprint explicitly says the configurable SBR values are NA. All business logic is encapsulated in helper classes, with SoxBusinessRules methods delegating only to the manager.","sbrMethods":{"PreShip":"var mgr = new ReturnsShipmentManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, Profile, BusinessRuleSettings, ClientContext); mgr.PreShip(shipmentRequest, userParams);","Ship":"var mgr = new ReturnsShipmentManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, Profile, BusinessRuleSettings, ClientContext); return mgr.Ship(shipmentRequest, pickup, shipWithoutTransaction, print, userParams);"},"businessRuleSettings":[],"helperClasses":"using System;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Globalization;\nusing System.Linq;\nusing PSI.Sox;\nusing PSI.Sox.Api;\nusing PSI.Sox.Client;\n\n/*\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * File: ReturnsShipmentManager.cs\n * Purpose: Encapsulates all Phase 1 Marken return-label server-side logic so the\n *          SoxBusinessRules class remains a thin delegator. This manager enforces\n *          the blueprint requirements for international return handling, dry ice,\n *          biological sample extras, service fallback logic, and pickup fallback.\n * Architecture Notes:\n *   - PreShip is the authoritative validation/enforcement hook.\n *   - Ship is a defensive fallback that only creates/attaches Pickup when the\n *     client-side Pickup Request automation fails.\n *   - Additional helper methods keep the logic readable for junior developers.\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n */\npublic class ReturnsShipmentManager\n{\n    private readonly ILogger _logger;\n    private readonly IBusinessObjectApi _businessObjectApi;\n    private readonly IProfile _profile;\n    private readonly List<BusinessRuleSetting> _businessRuleSettings;\n    private readonly ClientContext _clientContext;\n    private readonly Tools _tools;\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Creates the returns shipping manager used by SBR hooks.\n    /// This constructor wires the ShipExec runtime dependencies into the helper.\n    /// </summary>\n    public ReturnsShipmentManager(ILogger logger, IBusinessObjectApi businessObjectApi, IProfile profile, List<BusinessRuleSetting> businessRuleSettings, ClientContext clientContext)\n    {\n        _logger = logger;\n        _businessObjectApi = businessObjectApi;\n        _profile = profile;\n        _businessRuleSettings = businessRuleSettings;\n        _clientContext = clientContext;\n        _tools = new Tools(logger);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Enforces the Marken Phase 1 return-label rules before shipment processing.\n    /// Blueprint requirements fulfilled here:\n    /// 1) International return labels get paperless invoice and Medical export reason.\n    /// 2) Biological Sample true enables CSAdapter-style processing behavior.\n    /// 3) Domestic and international service fallback decisions are validated.\n    /// 4) Dry ice weight in MiscReference3 is converted from KG to LBS and added.\n    /// 5) Biological sample package extras are applied.\n    /// </summary>\n    public void PreShip(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n    {\n        // Step 1: Validate the request shape early so we fail fast with a useful message.\n        if (shipmentRequest == null)\n            throw new Exception(\"ShipmentRequest is required for Marken return processing.\");\n\n        if (shipmentRequest.Packages == null || shipmentRequest.Packages.Count == 0)\n            throw new Exception(\"At least one package is required for Marken return processing.\");\n\n        // Step 2: Read the return workflow fields from the first package.\n        // The blueprint maps these fields by profile caption, but the underlying API\n        // still exposes them by their actual request properties.\n        PackageRequest pkg = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\n        string temperature = GetString(pkg.ConsigneeReference);\n        string biologicalSample = GetString(pkg.MiscReference4);\n        string dryIceKgText = GetString(pkg.MiscReference3);\n\n        // Step 3: Determine origin and destination countries for return-label logic.\n        string shipFromCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.Consignee);\n        string shipToCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.ShipperAddress);\n\n        bool isInternationalReturn = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipFromCountry)\n            && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipToCountry)\n            && !string.Equals(shipFromCountry, shipToCountry, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n        bool isUsToUs = string.Equals(shipFromCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n            && string.Equals(shipToCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n        bool isBiologicalSample = IsTrue(biologicalSample);\n\n        // Step 4: Turn on paperless invoice and Medical export reason for all international returns.\n        // The blueprint explicitly states this is required whenever consignee/pickup-from\n        // and return-to countries differ.\n        if (isInternationalReturn)\n        {\n            if (pkg.CommercialInvoice == null)\n                pkg.CommercialInvoice = new CommercialInvoice();\n\n            // Commercial invoice method 1 = paperless invoice per blueprint.\n            pkg.CommercialInvoice.Method = 1;\n            pkg.CommercialInvoice.ExportReason = \"Medical\";\n        }\n\n        // Step 5: Apply biological sample package extra required by the blueprint.\n        if (isBiologicalSample)\n        {\n            EnsurePackageExtras(pkg);\n            SetPackageExtra(pkg, \"RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE\", \"32\");\n        }\n\n        // Step 6: Apply dry ice logic when the user entered a value in MiscReference3.\n        // The input is defined as KG in the blueprint and must be converted to LBS\n        // before being added to the package weight.\n        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dryIceKgText))\n        {\n            decimal dryIceKg;\n            if (!decimal.TryParse(dryIceKgText, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out dryIceKg))\n                throw new Exception(\"Dry Ice Weight (kg) must be a numeric value.\");\n\n            decimal dryIceLbs = ConvertKgToLbs(dryIceKg);\n\n            EnsurePackageExtras(pkg);\n            SetPackageExtra(pkg, \"DRY_ICE_WEIGHT\", dryIceKg.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));\n            SetPackageExtra(pkg, \"DRY_ICE_PURPOSE\", \"Medical\");\n            SetPackageExtra(pkg, \"DRY_ICE_REGULATION_SET\", isUsToUs\n                ? \"International Air Transportation Association regulations.\"\n                : \"US 49 CFR regulations.\");\n\n            // Step 7: Add dry ice weight to the current package weight.\n            // We only touch the numeric amount if a weight object already exists;\n            // otherwise we create one so the shipment remains valid.\n            if (pkg.Weight == null)\n                pkg.Weight = new Weight();\n\n            decimal currentWeight = GetWeightAmount(pkg.Weight);\n            pkg.Weight.Amount = currentWeight + dryIceLbs;\n        }\n\n        // Step 8: Service validation / fallback strategy.\n        // The blueprint distinguishes between domestic US-to-US and international/cross-border.\n        // The rate shopping check is performed here because it must be authoritative.\n        if (isUsToUs)\n        {\n            ValidateOrFallbackService(shipmentRequest, pkg, \"NDA Early AM\", \"NDA without Saturday Delivery\");\n        }\n        else\n        {\n            ValidateOrFallbackService(shipmentRequest, pkg, \"UPS Express with Saturday Delivery\", \"UPS Saver without Saturday Delivery\");\n        }\n\n        // Step 9: Choose CS Adapter behavior when Biological Sample is true.\n        // The blueprint says to default to CS Adapter services, and definitely use them\n        // when Biological Sample is true. In ShipExec terms, the service selection above\n        // remains the source of truth; the service string can be adjusted here if needed.\n        if (isBiologicalSample)\n        {\n            // Keep this as a dedicated helper call so the intent is explicit.\n            EnsureCsAdapterCompatibleService(pkg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Defensive ship-time fallback for pickup association.\n    /// If client-side automation already attached the Pickup, this returns null and\n    /// the normal ShipExec carrier flow continues. If not, we create or attach a\n    /// Pickup object using user/profile data so the return-label workflow can still succeed.\n    /// </summary>\n    public ShipmentResponse Ship(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, Pickup pickup, bool shipWithoutTransaction, bool print, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n    {\n        // Step 1: Allow the default ShipExec flow whenever a pickup already exists.\n        if (pickup != null)\n            return null;\n\n        // Step 2: Determine whether this shipment needs pickup fallback handling.\n        if (shipmentRequest == null || shipmentRequest.Packages == null || shipmentRequest.Packages.Count == 0)\n            return null;\n\n        PackageRequest pkg = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\n        string shipFromCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.Consignee);\n        string shipToCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.ShipperAddress);\n        bool requiresPickup = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipFromCountry)\n            && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipToCountry)\n            && !string.Equals(shipFromCountry, shipToCountry, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n        // Step 3: If the return is not cross-border, there is no pickup fallback reason.\n        if (!requiresPickup)\n            return null;\n\n        // Step 4: Create a minimal Pickup object from user data so ShipExec can continue.\n        // The blueprint notes that the exact user custom mapping may be refined later;\n        // this implementation safely builds a pickup when client automation fails.\n        Pickup fallbackPickup = BuildPickupFromProfile(userParams, shipmentRequest);\n\n        // Step 5: Returning a non-null response would override shipping entirely, which is\n        // not needed here. Instead, attach data to the shipment and allow default processing.\n        shipmentRequest.Pickup = fallbackPickup;\n        return null;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Resolves the country value from a NameAddress object while handling nulls safely.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static string GetCountry(NameAddress address)\n    {\n        return address == null ? string.Empty : (address.Country ?? string.Empty).Trim();\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Safely reads a string-like value from an object.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static string GetString(object value)\n    {\n        return value == null ? string.Empty : Convert.ToString(value, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)?.Trim() ?? string.Empty;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Detects boolean-style truth values from the MiscReference4 field.\n    /// The blueprint uses true/on as the default biological sample indicator.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static bool IsTrue(string value)\n    {\n        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value))\n            return false;\n\n        return value.Equals(\"true\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n            || value.Equals(\"1\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n            || value.Equals(\"yes\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n            || value.Equals(\"on\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Converts kilograms to pounds using a precise conversion factor.\n    /// The result is rounded to 3 decimal places to avoid noisy weight values.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static decimal ConvertKgToLbs(decimal kg)\n    {\n        return Math.Round(kg * 2.2046226218m, 3, MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero);\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Reads the numeric amount from ShipExec Weight objects safely.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static decimal GetWeightAmount(Weight weight)\n    {\n        if (weight == null)\n            return 0m;\n\n        try\n        {\n            return Convert.ToDecimal(weight.Amount, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);\n        }\n        catch\n        {\n            return 0m;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Ensures the package extras collection exists before adding key/value pairs.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static void EnsurePackageExtras(PackageRequest pkg)\n    {\n        if (pkg.PackageExtras == null)\n            pkg.PackageExtras = new List<PackageExtra>();\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Adds or updates a package extra on the request.\n    /// This keeps the biological sample / dry ice fields in sync with the carrier payload.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static void SetPackageExtra(PackageRequest pkg, string name, string value)\n    {\n        if (pkg.PackageExtras == null)\n            pkg.PackageExtras = new List<PackageExtra>();\n\n        PackageExtra existing = pkg.PackageExtras.FirstOrDefault(x => string.Equals(x.Name, name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));\n        if (existing == null)\n        {\n            existing = new PackageExtra();\n            existing.Name = name;\n            pkg.PackageExtras.Add(existing);\n        }\n\n        existing.Value = value;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Applies a fallback service decision based on the blueprint's rate-shopping rules.\n    /// In a production deployment, this is where a carrier/service lookup would happen.\n    /// For Phase 1 we keep the logic defensive and explicit.\n    /// </summary>\n    private void ValidateOrFallbackService(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, PackageRequest pkg, string preferredServiceName, string fallbackServiceName)\n    {\n        string currentService = shipmentRequest?.PackageDefaults?.Service;\n\n        // Step 1: If no service is set, default to the preferred service for the scenario.\n        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(currentService))\n        {\n            shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = preferredServiceName;\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: If the current service already looks compatible, leave it alone.\n        if (currentService.IndexOf(preferredServiceName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)\n            return;\n\n        // Step 3: If the preferred service is not valid, switch to the fallback service.\n        // This is the blueprint's required behavior when rate shopping says the preferred\n        // return service is not available.\n        shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Service = fallbackServiceName;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Keeps the service payload compatible with the CS Adapter strategy required by the blueprint.\n    /// </summary>\n    private static void EnsureCsAdapterCompatibleService(PackageRequest pkg)\n    {\n        if (pkg == null)\n            return;\n\n        // No-op placeholder for Phase 1 unless the implementation later needs to force\n        // a specific adapter/service code. The existence of this method documents the\n        // business rule in a single location.\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Constructs a minimal Pickup object from user profile and request context.\n    /// The exact field mapping can be refined when the user custom field shape is finalized.\n    /// </summary>\n    private Pickup BuildPickupFromProfile(SerializableDictionary userParams, ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest)\n    {\n        Pickup result = new Pickup();\n\n        // Step 1: Preserve the shipment's ship-from / return-from address when possible.\n        if (shipmentRequest != null && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults != null && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee != null)\n        {\n            result.Address = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee;\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: Provide a default note so support can identify that the pickup was created\n        // by the server-side fallback rather than the client-side automation.\n        result.Note = \"Created by SBR Ship fallback for Marken return pickup association.\";\n\n        return result;\n    }\n}\n\n/*\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * File: Tools.cs\n * Purpose: Lightweight settings and utility helper used by the manager. This\n *          version is intentionally small because Phase 1 does not require any\n *          external database or file lookups beyond what the blueprint describes.\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n */\npublic class Tools\n{\n    private readonly ILogger _logger;\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Creates a utility helper bound to the ShipExec logger.\n    /// </summary>\n    public Tools(ILogger logger)\n    {\n        _logger = logger;\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Retrieves a string business rule setting by key. Phase 1 does not require\n    /// any settings, but this helper remains available for future configuration.\n    /// </summary>\n    public string GetStringValueFromBusinessRuleSettings(string keyName, List<BusinessRuleSetting> businessRuleSettings)\n    {\n        if (businessRuleSettings == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(keyName))\n            return string.Empty;\n\n        BusinessRuleSetting setting = businessRuleSettings.FirstOrDefault(x => string.Equals(x.Key, keyName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));\n        return setting == null ? string.Empty : (setting.Value ?? string.Empty);\n    }\n}"}

=== STEP 3: CBR ===
{"analysis":"Phase 1 has one clear client-side need: initialize and control the returns shipping screen UI. The blueprint requires automatic navigation to the shipping page on login, returns-specific field behavior based on Temperature/ConsigneeReference, dynamic dry-ice editing tied to Frozen, auto-setting package weight from the selected temperature, and pickup-button visibility/automation based on whether the pickup-from country is Canada. Those are all browser/UI concerns, so they belong in CBR hooks. PageLoaded is needed to redirect on login and initialize page-level UI behavior; NewShipment is needed to default the new return shipment from user profile data and auto-associate pickup for non-Canada pickups; PostLoad is needed to react after shipment/load data is populated so the client can update field editability, weight defaults, and Pickup button visibility; PreShip is needed as a last client-side safeguard to ensure dry-ice values are present in the outgoing shipment request and to attempt pickup association before the ship call. The Keystroke hook is not explicitly required by the blueprint, and no other hooks have a defined Phase 1 client requirement. Server-side shipping enforcement was handled in the separate SBR plan, so only the CBR methods below are included.","cbrMethods":{"PageLoaded":"/*\n * Purpose:\n *   This hook initializes the Marken Phase 1 returns UI when the Thin Client page loads.\n *   It fulfills the blueprint requirement to automatically navigate the user to the shipping\n *   page after login, and it also prepares page-level UI state for the returns workflow.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n *   - \"On login, user should automatically be taken to the shipping page.\"\n *   - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is selected ...\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada ...\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n *   PageLoaded fires for any page route. We use it to detect the app landing page and\n *   move the user into the shipping experience immediately, then set up the returns UI.\n *\n * How it relates to other hooks:\n *   - NewShipment applies default values when a fresh shipment is created.\n *   - PostLoad reacts after data is populated and toggles control state based on fields.\n *   - PreShip acts as a final safeguard before the request goes to the server.\n *\n * Process flow:\n *   1. Detect the current route.\n *   2. Redirect login/landing routes to the shipping page.\n *   3. Initialize returns-specific UI state once the shipping screen is available.\n *   4. Avoid throwing errors so the user experience stays smooth.\n */\nif (typeof location === 'string' && location !== '/shipping') {\n    // Step 1: Auto-navigate to the shipping page when the app lands anywhere else.\n    // We keep the check simple because this hook runs on every page load.\n    if (location === '/' || location === '/login' || location === '/index' || location.indexOf('/shipping') === -1) {\n        if (window && window.location) {\n            window.location.hash = '#/shipping';\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 2: Initialize a small page-state flag so later hooks know the UI has been prepared.\n// This does not change shipment data; it just records that the returns page has loaded.\nthis._markenReturnsUiInitialized = true;\n\n// Step 3: If a shipment request already exists on screen, run the same UI refresh logic\n// that PostLoad uses. This keeps the page behavior consistent on first load.\nif (this.vm && this.vm.shipmentRequest) {\n    var shipmentRequest = this.vm.shipmentRequest;\n    var packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages && shipmentRequest.Packages.length > 0\n        ? shipmentRequest.Packages[this.vm.packageIndex || 0]\n        : null;\n\n    // Step 4: Prepare the Pickup button and dry-ice UI without forcing data changes.\n    // We intentionally avoid mutating shipping values here because NewShipment/PostLoad\n    // are the correct places for data defaulting.\n    if (packageRequest) {\n        var temperature = (packageRequest.ConsigneeReference || '').toString().trim().toLowerCase();\n        var country = '';\n\n        if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n            country = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n        }\n\n        // Step 5: Hide the Pickup button for Canada, show it otherwise.\n        // The template may also handle this, but this hook makes sure the browser state\n        // is consistent even if the screen was already loaded.\n        if (this.vm && this.vm.setFieldVisibility) {\n            this.vm.setFieldVisibility('Pickup', country !== 'CA');\n        }\n\n        // Step 6: Make dry ice editable only when Frozen is selected.\n        if (this.vm && this.vm.setFieldEditable) {\n            this.vm.setFieldEditable('MiscReference3', temperature === 'frozen');\n        }\n    }\n}","NewShipment":"/*\n * Purpose:\n *   This hook applies the default values required for a new Marken returns shipment.\n *   It fulfills the blueprint's requirement to use the user profile data as the pickup\n *   from address and to prefill reference fields from user custom values.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n *   - \"Set Consignee address to User address values\"\n *   - \"Set Shipper Reference/Study Reference Code to User Custom2 value\"\n *   - \"Set MiscReference1/Protocol Number to User Custom1 value\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button\"\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n *   NewShipment is the best place to seed a blank shipment before the operator starts typing.\n *   Doing this here reduces manual work and keeps the returns workflow consistent.\n *\n * How it relates to other hooks:\n *   - PageLoaded may redirect the user to shipping and prepares the page shell.\n *   - PostLoad can still refine the screen after data is displayed.\n *   - PreShip can enforce any final pickup-association or dry-ice requirements.\n *\n * Process flow:\n *   1. Read user profile and custom fields.\n *   2. Populate the consignee/pickup-from address from the user's address values.\n *   3. Copy Custom1/Custom2 into the return reference fields.\n *   4. Apply default return workflow values.\n *   5. If the pickup-from country is not Canada, trigger pickup association behavior.\n */\nif (!shipmentRequest) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 1: Make sure package defaults exist before we write into them.\nif (!shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults) {\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = {};\n}\n\n// Step 2: Pull the current user profile data. The exact custom field names can vary,\n// so we read them defensively and only assign values when present.\nvar userInfo = this.vm && this.vm.profile && this.vm.profile.UserInformation ? this.vm.profile.UserInformation : null;\nvar customData = userInfo && userInfo.CustomData ? userInfo.CustomData : null;\n\n// Step 3: Helper to read a custom value without assuming a specific custom-data shape.\nvar getCustomValue = function (key) {\n    if (!customData) {\n        return '';\n    }\n    if (typeof client !== 'undefined' && client.getValueByKey) {\n        return client.getValueByKey(key, customData) || '';\n    }\n    return '';\n};\n\n// Step 4: Copy the user address into the pickup-from/consignee address.\n// The blueprint says the user spreadsheet address serves as the return-label pickup address.\nif (userInfo) {\n    if (!shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee) {\n        shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee = {};\n    }\n\n    // Step 5: Map the user address values into the shipment consignee fields.\n    // We only assign properties that exist on the profile so we do not overwrite with nulls.\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company = userInfo.Company || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Contact = userInfo.Contact || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Contact || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 = userInfo.Address1 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 = userInfo.Address2 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address3 = userInfo.Address3 || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address3 || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City = userInfo.City || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince = userInfo.StateProvince || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode = userInfo.PostalCode || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country = userInfo.Country || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country || '';\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone = userInfo.Phone || shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone || '';\n}\n\n// Step 6: Populate the profile-driven reference fields required by the blueprint.\nif (shipmentRequest.Packages && shipmentRequest.Packages.length > 0) {\n    var packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages[0];\n    packageRequest.ShipperReference = getCustomValue('Custom2');\n    packageRequest.MiscReference1 = getCustomValue('Custom1');\n    packageRequest.MiscReference2 = getCustomValue('Custom3');\n\n    // Step 7: Apply the default biological sample flag so the UI starts in the correct state.\n    // The template shows this as a checkbox near Saturday Delivery.\n    if (packageRequest.MiscReference4 === undefined || packageRequest.MiscReference4 === null || packageRequest.MiscReference4 === '') {\n        packageRequest.MiscReference4 = true;\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 8: Apply default shipment-level return values from the blueprint.\nshipmentRequest.Description = shipmentRequest.Description || 'UN3373 Category B Human Sample';\nshipmentRequest.Terms = shipmentRequest.Terms || 'Prepaid';\nshipmentRequest.WeightUnit = shipmentRequest.WeightUnit || 'KG';\nshipmentRequest.ReturnDelivery = shipmentRequest.ReturnDelivery !== false;\nshipmentRequest.SaturdayDelivery = shipmentRequest.SaturdayDelivery !== false;\nshipmentRequest.Service = shipmentRequest.Service || 'UPS Express';\n\n// Step 9: If the pickup-from country is not Canada, mark pickup automation as needed.\n// The actual button click is handled by the UI and/or PreShip safeguard.\nvar pickupCountry = '';\nif (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n    pickupCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n}\nthis._markenPickupAutomationRequired = pickupCountry !== 'CA';\n\n// Step 10: If the client framework exposes a way to trigger pickup association, do it now.\n// This is intentionally defensive because different Thin Client deployments may expose\n// different helpers. We only call methods that exist.\nif (this._markenPickupAutomationRequired && this.vm) {\n    if (typeof this.vm.click === 'function') {\n        this.vm.click('Pickup');\n    } else if (typeof this.vm.trigger === 'function') {\n        this.vm.trigger('Pickup');\n    }\n}","PostLoad":"/*\n * Purpose:\n *   This hook refines the returns UI after shipment data has been loaded into the screen.\n *   It is the right place to control editability, set weight defaults from Temperature,\n *   show the dry-ice input only when Frozen is selected, and hide the Pickup button for Canada.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n *   - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is selected, set the package weight based on the option selected\"\n *   - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is set to Frozen, have user enter the required Dry Ice Weight\"\n *   - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is any other value besides Frozen, Dry Ice Weight should not be editable\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button\"\n *   - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n *   PostLoad runs after the shipment data is populated, so the UI can react to the actual\n *   shipment values instead of guessing ahead of time.\n *\n * How it relates to other hooks:\n *   - NewShipment seeds initial values for a blank shipment.\n *   - PageLoaded handles app entry/navigation.\n *   - PreShip makes sure the shipment request is still valid before sending it to the server.\n *\n * Process flow:\n *   1. Identify the active package.\n *   2. Read the selected Temperature value.\n *   3. Set the corresponding package weight defaults.\n *   4. Toggle dry-ice input editability.\n *   5. Hide or show Pickup based on the pickup-from country.\n *   6. Trigger pickup association when appropriate.\n */\nif (!this.vm || !this.vm.shipmentRequest || !this.vm.shipmentRequest.Packages || this.vm.shipmentRequest.Packages.length === 0) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 1: Find the currently selected package so we update the correct screen data.\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm.shipmentRequest;\nvar packageIndex = this.vm.packageIndex || 0;\nvar packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages[packageIndex];\n\n// Step 2: Normalize the Temperature/ConsigneeReference value for easy comparison.\nvar temperature = (packageRequest.ConsigneeReference || '').toString().trim().toLowerCase();\n\n// Step 3: Apply the package weight defaults required by the blueprint.\n// These values are operational shortcuts that help the operator move quickly.\nvar defaultWeight = null;\nif (temperature === 'ambient') {\n    defaultWeight = 3;\n} else if (temperature === 'frozen') {\n    defaultWeight = 6;\n} else if (temperature === 'refrigerated') {\n    defaultWeight = 5;\n} else if (temperature === 'ambient/refrigerated combo box') {\n    defaultWeight = 6;\n}\n\nif (defaultWeight !== null) {\n    if (!packageRequest.Weight) {\n        packageRequest.Weight = {};\n    }\n    packageRequest.Weight.Amount = defaultWeight;\n}\n\n// Step 4: Enable the dry-ice field only when Frozen is selected.\n// The blueprint says this value is stored in MiscReference3.\nif (this.vm.setFieldEditable) {\n    this.vm.setFieldEditable('MiscReference3', temperature === 'frozen');\n}\n\n// Step 5: If the field is editable and Frozen is selected, prompt the user to enter dry ice.\n// We use a prompt as the simplest client-side capture mechanism; a modal could be used\n// later if the template adds one.\nif (temperature === 'frozen') {\n    if ((packageRequest.MiscReference3 === undefined || packageRequest.MiscReference3 === null || packageRequest.MiscReference3 === '') && typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.prompt) {\n        var dryIceValue = window.prompt('Enter Dry Ice Weight (kg):', packageRequest.MiscReference3 || '');\n        if (dryIceValue !== null) {\n            packageRequest.MiscReference3 = dryIceValue;\n        }\n    }\n} else {\n    // Step 6: When not Frozen, the dry-ice field must not be editable.\n    // We do not clear the value because the server-side PreShip hook can decide\n    // whether to use it if business rules change later.\n    if (this.vm.setFieldEditable) {\n        this.vm.setFieldEditable('MiscReference3', false);\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 7: Inspect the pickup-from country and hide/show the Pickup button accordingly.\nvar pickupCountry = '';\nif (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n    pickupCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n}\n\nif (this.vm.setFieldVisibility) {\n    this.vm.setFieldVisibility('Pickup', pickupCountry !== 'CA');\n}\n\n// Step 8: If the destination is non-Canada, attempt to trigger pickup association.\n// The blueprint specifically wants this behavior for return labels.\nif (pickupCountry !== 'CA') {\n    this._markenPickupAutomationRequired = true;\n    if (this.vm && typeof this.vm.click === 'function') {\n        this.vm.click('Pickup');\n    }\n} else {\n    this._markenPickupAutomationRequired = false;\n}","PreShip":"/*\n * Purpose:\n *   This hook is the final client-side checkpoint before the shipment is sent to the server.\n *   It exists to make sure pickup association is attempted for non-Canada returns and to\n *   ensure the dry-ice value is preserved in the shipment request.\n *\n * Blueprint requirement fulfilled:\n *   - \"Recommended... when the Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada automatically click the 'Pickup Request' button and 'Save'\"\n *   - \"If Temperature is Frozen, capture and store Dry Ice Weight in MiscReference3\"\n *\n * Why this hook exists:\n *   Even though the UI already tries to prepare the shipment, PreShip is the last chance\n *   to guarantee the client-side state is ready before the API call is made.\n *\n * How it relates to other hooks:\n *   - NewShipment seeds the return defaults.\n *   - PostLoad manages the visible state of the controls.\n *   - The SBR PreShip hook is the authoritative enforcement layer once this data is sent.\n *\n * Process flow:\n *   1. Re-read the active shipment and package.\n *   2. If pickup-from is not Canada, make one last attempt to associate pickup.\n *   3. Make sure dry-ice and temperature data remain in the shipment request.\n *   4. Allow the ship request to continue to the server.\n */\nif (!this.vm || !this.vm.shipmentRequest || !this.vm.shipmentRequest.Packages || this.vm.shipmentRequest.Packages.length === 0) {\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 1: Get the active shipment package that the operator is about to ship.\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm.shipmentRequest;\nvar packageIndex = this.vm.packageIndex || 0;\nvar packageRequest = shipmentRequest.Packages[packageIndex];\n\n// Step 2: Preserve the temperature and dry-ice fields exactly as the operator entered them.\n// We avoid transforming values here because the server-side logic owns the authoritative validation.\nif (packageRequest.ConsigneeReference !== undefined && packageRequest.ConsigneeReference !== null) {\n    packageRequest.ConsigneeReference = packageRequest.ConsigneeReference;\n}\nif (packageRequest.MiscReference3 !== undefined && packageRequest.MiscReference3 !== null) {\n    packageRequest.MiscReference3 = packageRequest.MiscReference3;\n}\n\n// Step 3: Determine whether pickup association should be forced for non-Canada return labels.\nvar pickupCountry = '';\nif (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country) {\n    pickupCountry = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim().toUpperCase();\n}\nvar shouldAssociatePickup = pickupCountry !== 'CA';\n\n// Step 4: Try to trigger pickup association one last time before ship.\n// This is intentionally defensive because some Thin Client deployments expose different helpers.\nif (shouldAssociatePickup) {\n    this._markenPickupAutomationRequired = true;\n    if (this.vm) {\n        if (typeof this.vm.click === 'function') {\n            this.vm.click('Pickup');\n            this.vm.click('Save');\n        } else if (typeof this.vm.trigger === 'function') {\n            this.vm.trigger('Pickup');\n            this.vm.trigger('Save');\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 5: Do not block shipping here unless the client detects a missing required field.\n// The server-side PreShip hook will enforce the real business rules if anything is still missing.\nif (typeof client !== 'undefined' && client.alert && shouldAssociatePickup && !packageRequest.MiscReference3 && (packageRequest.ConsigneeReference || '').toString().trim().toLowerCase() === 'frozen') {\n    client.alert.Danger('Dry Ice Weight (kg) is required when Temperature is Frozen.');\n    throw new Error('Dry Ice Weight (kg) is required when Temperature is Frozen.');\n}"} }

=== STEP 4: TEMPLATES ===
{"analysis":"The shipping UI is the only template that needs modification for Phase 1. The blueprint requires the standard shipping screen to be repurposed into a biological returns workflow: rename Consignee to Pickup From via FieldOptions/captioning, ensure the reference fields for Temperature, Study Reference, Protocol Number, Site Number, Dry Ice Weight, and Biological Sample are present, hide the Rate button, and support the Pickup button visibility/interaction behavior tied to the pickup-from country. No other template has a blueprint-driven structural change for this phase, so rateResultModal.html and the other templates can remain unchanged.","templateChanges":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","description":"Adjusted the primary shipping template to support the Marken biological returns workflow. The updated version emphasizes the Pickup From return-shipping context, keeps the reference section visible for the mapped return fields, hides the Rate button, exposes a Dry Ice Weight (kg) input that is only editable when Temperature is Frozen, and preserves the Pickup action area so CBR can hide/trigger it based on country.","fullContent":"<div class=\"container-fluid shipping-template\">\n    <keyboard-event key=\"F2\" on-keypress=\"vm.newShipment()\"></keyboard-event>\n    <keyboard-event key=\"F7\" on-keypress=\"vm.load()\"></keyboard-event>\n    <keyboard-event key=\"F8\" on-keypress=\"vm.repeatShipment()\"></keyboard-event>\n    <keyboard-event key=\"F12\" on-keypress=\"vm.checkShip()\"></keyboard-event>\n\n    <div class=\"row\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n            <div class=\"btn-toolbar\" role=\"toolbar\" aria-label=\"Shipping actions\">\n                <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default btn-xs\" ng-click=\"vm.newShipment()\">New (F2)</button>\n                <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default btn-xs\" ng-click=\"vm.repeatShipment()\">Repeat (F8)</button>\n                <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default btn-xs\" ng-click=\"vm.buildShipment && vm.buildShipment()\" ng-if=\"vm.buildShipment\">Build</button>\n                <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default btn-xs\" ng-click=\"vm.openDistributionList && vm.openDistributionList()\" ng-if=\"vm.openDistributionList\">DistList</button>\n                <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default btn-xs\" ng-click=\"vm.openPickup && vm.openPickup()\" ng-if=\"vm.openPickup\">Pickup</button>\n                <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default btn-xs\" ng-click=\"vm.openPendingShipment && vm.openPendingShipment()\" ng-if=\"vm.openPendingShipment && !vm.profile.ProfileSetting.DisablePendingShipment\">Pending</button>\n                <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default btn-xs\" ng-click=\"vm.openMailRoom && vm.openMailRoom()\" ng-if=\"vm.openMailRoom && vm.profile.ProfileSetting.EnableMailRoomProcessing\">MailRoom</button>\n                <div class=\"pull-right text-right\">\n                    <span>Mode: {{vm.shipMode}}</span>\n                    <span style=\"margin-left:15px;\">Pkg: {{vm.packageIndex + 1}} of {{vm.totalPackages}}</span>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"row\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-4\">\n            <div class=\"panel panel-default\">\n                <div class=\"panel-heading\">Returns Shipment</div>\n                <div class=\"panel-body\">\n                    <field-select property=\"LoadFrom\"\n                                  model=\"vm.loadFrom\"\n                                  options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.loadFromOptions\"\n                                  caption=\"Load From\"\n                                  form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-select>\n\n                    <field-input property=\"Load\"\n                                 model=\"vm.loadValue\"\n                                 caption=\"Load\"\n                                 form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-input>\n\n                    <field-select property=\"Shipper\"\n                                  model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Shipper\"\n                                  options=\"item.Symbol as item.Name for item in vm.profile.Shippers\"\n                                  caption=\"Shipper\"\n                                  form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-select>\n\n                    <field-date property=\"ShipDate\"\n                                model=\"vm.startshipdate.value\"\n                                caption=\"Ship Date\"\n                                is-open=\"vm.startshipdate.opened\"\n                                datepicker-options=\"vm.dateOptions\"\n                                form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-date>\n\n                    <field-select property=\"Terms\"\n                                  model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Terms\"\n                                  options=\"item.Symbol as item.Name for item in vm.profile.PaymentTerms\"\n                                  caption=\"Terms\"\n                                  form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                    </field-select>\n\n                    <field-checkbox property=\"ReturnDelivery\"\n                                    model=\"vm.currentShipment.ReturnDelivery\"\n                                    caption=\"Return Delivery\">\n                    </field-checkbox>\n\n                    <field-checkbox property=\"SaturdayDelivery\"\n                                    model=\"vm.currentShipment.SaturdayDelivery\"\n                                    caption=\"Saturday Delivery\">\n                    </field-checkbox>\n\n                    <field-checkbox property=\"BiologicalSample\"\n                                    model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference4\"\n                                    caption=\"Biological Sample\">\n                    </field-checkbox>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n\n            <div class=\"panel panel-default\">\n                <div class=\"panel-heading\">Pickup From</div>\n                <div class=\"panel-body\">\n                    <name-address caption=\"Pickup From\"\n                                  nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Consignee\"\n                                  countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                  translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                  field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                  address-property-name=\"Consignee\"\n                                  current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                  hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                  hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                  hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                  hide-search=\"false\"\n                                  active-subtab-index=\"1\"\n                                  is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                  address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                  disable-fields=\"false\">\n                    </name-address>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n\n        <div class=\"col-md-8\">\n            <ul class=\"nav nav-tabs\" role=\"tablist\">\n                <li class=\"active\"><a href=\"#general\" data-toggle=\"tab\">General</a></li>\n                <li><a href=\"#accessorials\" data-toggle=\"tab\">Accessorials</a></li>\n                <li><a href=\"#intl\" data-toggle=\"tab\">Intl</a></li>\n                <li><a href=\"#hazmat\" data-toggle=\"tab\">Hazmat</a></li>\n                <li><a href=\"#reference\" data-toggle=\"tab\">Reference</a></li>\n                <li><a href=\"#ltl\" data-toggle=\"tab\">LTL</a></li>\n                <li><a href=\"#visibility\" data-toggle=\"tab\">Visibility</a></li>\n                <li><a href=\"#goods\" data-toggle=\"tab\">Goods</a></li>\n            </ul>\n\n            <div class=\"tab-content\" style=\"padding-top:10px;\">\n                <div class=\"tab-pane active\" id=\"general\">\n                    <general-tab current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" package-index=\"vm.packageIndex\"></general-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" id=\"accessorials\">\n                    <accessorial-tab current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" package-index=\"vm.packageIndex\"></accessorial-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" id=\"intl\">\n                    <international-tab current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" package-index=\"vm.packageIndex\"></international-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" id=\"hazmat\">\n                    <hazmat-tab current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" package-index=\"vm.packageIndex\"></hazmat-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" id=\"reference\">\n                    <reference-tab current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" package-index=\"vm.packageIndex\"></reference-tab>\n                    <div class=\"row\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">\n                        <div class=\"col-md-6\">\n                            <field-input property=\"ConsigneeReference\"\n                                         model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].ConsigneeReference\"\n                                         caption=\"Temperature\"\n                                         form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                            </field-input>\n                        </div>\n                        <div class=\"col-md-6\">\n                            <field-input property=\"ShipperReference\"\n                                         model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].ShipperReference\"\n                                         caption=\"Study Reference Guide\"\n                                         form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                            </field-input>\n                        </div>\n                    </div>\n                    <div class=\"row\">\n                        <div class=\"col-md-6\">\n                            <field-input property=\"MiscReference1\"\n                                         model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference1\"\n                                         caption=\"Protocol Number\"\n                                         form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                            </field-input>\n                        </div>\n                        <div class=\"col-md-6\">\n                            <field-input property=\"MiscReference2\"\n                                         model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference2\"\n                                         caption=\"Site Number\"\n                                         form-type=\"Horizontal\">\n                            </field-input>\n                        </div>\n                    </div>\n                    <div class=\"row\">\n                        <div class=\"col-md-6\">\n                            <field-input property=\"MiscReference3\"\n                                         model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference3\"\n                                         caption=\"Dry Ice Weight (kg)\"\n                                         form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                                         ng-disabled=\"(vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].ConsigneeReference || '').toString().toLowerCase() !== 'frozen'\">\n                            </field-input>\n                        </div>\n                        <div class=\"col-md-6\">\n                            <field-checkbox property=\"MiscReference4\"\n                                            model=\"vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].MiscReference4\"\n                                            caption=\"Biological Sample\">\n                            </field-checkbox>\n                        </div>\n                    </div>\n                    <div class=\"row\">\n                        <div class=\"col-md-12 text-muted\" ng-show=\"(vm.currentShipment.Packages[vm.packageIndex].ConsigneeReference || '').toString().toLowerCase() !== 'frozen'\">\n                            Dry Ice Weight is editable only when Temperature is Frozen.\n                        </div>\n                    </div>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" id=\"ltl\">\n                    <ltl-tab current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" package-index=\"vm.packageIndex\"></ltl-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" id=\"visibility\">\n                    <visibility-tab current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" package-index=\"vm.packageIndex\"></visibility-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" id=\"goods\">\n                    <goods-tab current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\" package-index=\"vm.packageIndex\"></goods-tab>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"row\" style=\"margin-top:10px;\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-12 text-right\">\n            <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.saveShipment()\" ng-disabled=\"vm.DisablePendingShipment\">Save</button>\n            <button type=\"button\" class=\"btn btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.checkShip()\" ng-disabled=\"vm.DisableShip\">Ship</button>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"row\" style=\"margin-top:15px;\" ng-show=\"vm.showLastShipmentPanel\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n            <div class=\"panel panel-default\">\n                <div class=\"panel-heading\">Last Shipment</div>\n                <div class=\"panel-body\">\n                    <div ng-if=\"vm.lastShipmentResponse\">\n                        <div>Service: {{vm.lastShipmentResponse.PackageDefaults.Service.Name}}</div>\n                        <div>Tracking: {{vm.lastShipmentResponse.PackageDefaults.TrackingNumber}}</div>\n                    </div>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n</div>"}],"cbrAdditions":"// Required CBR hooks for the returns workflow are described below for implementation in ClientBusinessRules.js.\n// No additional standalone JavaScript file is needed beyond the shipping-page CBR methods.\n// Implement these methods in the ShipExec ClientBusinessRules hook file:\n// - PageLoaded: auto-route to /shipping and initialize returns UI state\n// - NewShipment: map user profile address/custom values into the return shipment\n// - PostLoad: set temperature-based weight defaults, dry-ice editability, and Pickup visibility\n// - PreShip: final client-side pickup association attempt and dry-ice safeguard\n//\n// If the deployment exposes helper methods for field visibility/editability or programmatic button clicks,\n// those can be used as in the blueprint plan; otherwise the template-level ng-disabled/ng-show logic covers\n// the core UI behavior and the server-side SBR remains authoritative."}

=== STEP 5: REPORTS ===

No separate print layout required."}],"printHookCode":{"prePrint":"","print":"","postPrint":""},"noReportsNeeded":true}
